description: Classification of the fire zone or control area type to which the maximum quantity limit applies. NFPA 400 and IFC allow higher MAQ in sprinklered buildings versus unprotected control areas. Drives differentiated quantity limits in SAP EWM storage type configuration.

business_glossary_term: Globally Harmonized System (GHS) Hazard Statement Codes
description: Comma-separated list of GHS H-statement codes applicable to chemicals governed by this rule (e.g., H225,H302,H314). Drives SDS Section 2 alignment and label generation. Used in REACH and CLP regulatory reporting.

description: Indicates whether electrical grounding and bonding equipment is required at the storage location for chemicals governed by this rule. Mandatory for flammable liquids with flash points below 100°F to prevent static discharge ignition per NFPA 77 and OSHA standards.

Primary GHS/UN hazard class of the chemical governed by this rule (e.g., Class 3 Flammable Liquids, Class 5.1 Oxidizing Substances, Class 8 Corrosives, Class 6.1 Toxic Substances). Aligns with UN Model Regulations and GHS classification. description: Secondary hazard classification or division providing additional specificity within the primary hazard class (e.g., Division 1.1 for mass explosion risk, Packing Group I/II/III, sub-risk 6.1 on a Class 3 flammable). Captures multi-hazard chemical profiles per GHS and UN regulations.
reference: GHS Purple Book; UN Model Regulations; DOT 49 CFR 172.101

description: Primary hazardous consequence that would result from co-locating the two incompatible hazard classes defined in this rule. Used in risk assessment, PHA/HAZOP documentation, and emergency response planning. Informs the severity weighting in automated putaway violation alerts.
reference: OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119; NFPA 400; EPA RMP 40 CFR Part 68; HAZOP methodology

business_glossary_term: Incompatible Hazard Class — Side A (Segregation Matrix)
description: First hazard class in the incompatible pair defined by this segregation rule. Together with incompatible_hazard_class_b, forms the segregation matrix entry that prohibits co-location of chemicals from these two hazard classes in the same storage zone. Aligns with NFPA 400 Table 10.3.2 and UN segregation table.
reference: NFPA 400; UN Model Regulations Chapter 7.2; IMDG Code; DOT 49 CFR 177.848

business_glossary_term: Incompatible Hazard Class — Side B (Segregation Matrix)
description: Second hazard class in the incompatible pair defined by this segregation rule. Together with incompatible_hazard_class_a, forms the segregation matrix entry. The automated putaway validation engine in SAP EWM checks both directions of this pair to prevent co-location of incompatible chemicals.
reference: NFPA 400; UN Model Regulations Chapter 7.2; IMDG Code; DOT 49 CFR 177.848

description: Timestamp when this hazmat storage rule record was last updated. Supports change detection in downstream ETL pipelines and provides audit trail for rule modifications. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per enterprise data standards.
reference: ISO 9001; SOX; Intelex EHS Management; SAP EWM

description: Date on which this storage rule was last reviewed for continued regulatory accuracy and operational relevance. ISO 14001 and ISO 45001 require periodic review of EHS controls. Triggers a review workflow in Intelex EHS Management when overdue.
reference: ISO 14001; ISO 45001; OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119; ACC Responsible Care

business_glossary_term: Maximum Storage Quantity Per Fire Zone (Kilograms)
description: Maximum allowable quantity in kilograms of chemicals governed by this rule that may be stored within a single fire zone or control area. Derived from NFPA 400 maximum allowable quantities (MAQ) tables and local fire code. Enforced by SAP EWM inventory capacity checks.
reference: NFPA 400 Table 4.3.1; IFC Chapter 50; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106

business_glossary_term: Maximum Storage Quantity Per Fire Zone (Liters)
description: Maximum allowable quantity in liters of liquid chemicals governed by this rule that may be stored within a single fire zone or control area. Complements the kilogram-based limit for liquid hazmat categories where volume is the regulatory basis (e.g., NFPA 30 flammable liquids).
reference: NFPA 30; NFPA 400; IFC Chapter 50; EPA 40 CFR Part 112

description: Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for chemicals governed by this rule. Exceeding this threshold may cause decomposition, polymerization, or ignition. Monitored via Aveva PI System historian and triggers automated EHS alerts in Intelex.
reference: GHS; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106; NFPA 400; SDS Section 7

description: Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for chemicals governed by this rule. Below this threshold, the chemical may solidify, become unstable, or lose efficacy. Monitored via Aveva PI System historian for real-time compliance alerting.
reference: GHS; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106; ISO 9001; SDS Section 7

description: Date by which this storage rule must next be reviewed for regulatory currency and operational accuracy. Calculated based on review frequency policy (typically annual for high-hazard rules). Drives compliance calendar in Intelex EHS Management.
reference: ISO 14001; ISO 45001; OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119; ACC Responsible Care

business_glossary_term: National Fire Protection Association (NFPA) Fire Diamond Flammability Rating
description: NFPA 704 fire diamond flammability rating (0–4) for chemicals governed by this rule. Rating 4 indicates materials that will rapidly vaporize at normal pressure and temperature. Used in fire zone maximum quantity calculations and emergency response planning.

business_glossary_term: National Fire Protection Association (NFPA) Fire Diamond Health Rating
description: NFPA 704 fire diamond health hazard rating (0–4) for chemicals governed by this rule. Rating 4 indicates materials that can cause death or major residual injury on short exposure. Used in PPE requirements and emergency response planning.

business_glossary_term: National Fire Protection Association (NFPA) Fire Diamond Reactivity Rating
description: NFPA 704 fire diamond instability/reactivity rating (0–4) for chemicals governed by this rule. Rating 4 indicates materials capable of detonation or explosive decomposition at normal temperatures and pressures.

business_glossary_term: National Fire Protection Association (NFPA) Fire Diamond Special Hazard Code
description: NFPA 704 special hazard symbol code for chemicals governed by this rule (e.g., W = water-reactive, OX = oxidizer, SA = simple asphyxiant, BIO = biological hazard, CRY = cryogenic). Governs special storage precautions and emergency response procedures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: W|OX|SA|BIO|COR|POI|RAD|CRY|ACID|ALK — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

business_glossary_term: Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) Regulatory Citation
description: Specific OSHA regulatory citation (CFR part and section) that mandates or informs this storage rule (e.g., '29 CFR 1910.119 PSM', '29 CFR 1910.106 Flammable Liquids', '29 CFR 1910.1200 HazCom'). Used in PSM compliance documentation and OSHA inspection readiness.

UN packing group designation (I = great danger, II = medium danger, III = minor danger) for the hazardous material governed by this rule. Determines packaging requirements for transport and influences storage quantity limits and segregation requirements.
reference: UN Model Regulations; DOT 49 CFR 173; IMDG Code; IATA DGR

business_glossary_term: Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) Regulation Reference
description: REACH regulation article or annex reference applicable to this storage rule (e.g., 'REACH Annex XVII Restriction Entry 3', 'REACH Article 56 Authorization'). Required for EU operations and ECHA compliance reporting. Populated for chemicals on the SVHC candidate list.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; ECHA; CLP Regulation (EC) No 1272/2008

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this storage rule within the enterprise rule catalog (e.g., HSR-001, FLAM-OX-SEG-01). Used as the human-readable business key in SAP EWM putaway strategies and EHS management systems.
reference: SAP EWM; Intelex EHS Management; NFPA 400; GHS

description: Full narrative description of the storage rule, including the rationale for the segregation or storage condition requirement, the chemical hazard interaction being prevented, and any operational guidance for warehouse personnel.
reference: GHS; NFPA 400; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106; TSCA; REACH

description: Descriptive short name for the storage rule (e.g., 'Flammable-Oxidizer Segregation', 'Corrosive-Reactive Incompatibility'). Used in warehouse management UI labels and EHS compliance reports.

Current lifecycle status of the storage rule record. 'active' rules are enforced in automated putaway validation. 'superseded' rules have been replaced by a newer version. 'under_review' rules are pending regulatory or internal re-assessment.
reference: ISO 9001; Intelex EHS Management; SAP EWM

description: Version identifier for this storage rule record (e.g., v1.0, v2.3). Supports rule versioning when regulatory updates require rule revisions. Enables audit trail of rule changes over time and ensures warehouse systems reference the current approved version.
reference: ISO 9001 Document Control; Intelex EHS Management; SAP EWM

description: Comma-separated list of SDS section numbers that provide the source data for this storage rule (e.g., '7,8,10,14'). Section 7 = Handling and Storage, Section 8 = Exposure Controls, Section 10 = Reactivity, Section 14 = Transport. Enables traceability from rule to SDS source documentation per GHS.
reference: GHS Purple Book SDS Format; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200 Appendix D; REACH Article 31

description: Minimum physical separation distance in meters required between storage locations holding chemicals from the two incompatible hazard classes defined in this rule. Used by SAP EWM bin assignment logic to enforce spatial separation within the warehouse or tank farm.
reference: NFPA 400; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106; UN Model Regulations

description: Type of physical storage location required for chemicals governed by this rule. Drives SAP EWM storage type determination during putaway. 'tank_farm' applies to bulk liquid chemicals. 'refrigerated_room' applies to temperature-sensitive hazmat. Validated against warehouse master data.
reference: NFPA 400; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106; SAP EWM Storage Type Configuration

description: UN four-digit identification number for the hazardous substance or article governed by this rule (e.g., UN1203 for gasoline, UN1824 for sodium hydroxide solution). Used in DOT transport documentation, emergency response, and cross-referencing with the UN Model Regulations segregation table.
reference: UN Model Regulations; DOT 49 CFR 172.101; IMDG Code; IATA DGR

description: Required ventilation classification for storage areas holding chemicals governed by this rule. 'local_exhaust' indicates dedicated fume extraction is required. 'explosion_proof' indicates ATEX/NEC-rated ventilation systems are mandatory. Aligns with OSHA industrial hygiene requirements and NFPA 30.

Unique surrogate identifier for the storage bin master record in the SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) system. Serves as the primary key for all bin-level inventory tracking and directed putaway/picking operations.
Foreign key linking to inventory.hazmat_storage_rule. Business justification: Hazardous storage rules are defined centrally; link bin to rule and remove duplicated class info.
Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_location. Business justification: Storage bin belongs to a warehouse location; replace free‑text warehouse_number with FK for proper hierarchy.
Aisle identifier within the warehouse where the storage bin is physically located. Used for warehouse navigation, slotting optimization, and pick path sequencing.

Comma-separated list of SAP material type codes (e.g., ROH for raw materials, FERT for finished goods, HALB for semi-finished, VERP for packaging) permitted to be stored in this bin. Enforces material segregation rules for chemical compatibility and regulatory compliance.
reference: SAP EWM; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119

description: Type of material handling equipment required to access this storage bin. Determines equipment assignment during transfer order creation and supports warehouse resource planning. Critical for safety compliance in chemical warehouses where certain hazmat bins require specialized handling equipment.

Alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the physical storage bin within a warehouse number and storage type. Corresponds to the SAP EWM Lgpla field. Used in directed putaway, picking, and bin-level inventory queries.

Free-text description of the storage bin providing additional context about its physical characteristics, special use restrictions, or operational notes. May include information about dedicated chemical families, special handling requirements, or historical usage notes.

Human-readable descriptive name or label for the storage bin, used in warehouse operations, signage, and reporting. May include aisle, row, and level descriptors (e.g., 'Aisle-A Row-03 Level-02') to aid physical navigation.

Current operational lifecycle status of the storage bin. Active bins are available for putaway and picking. Blocked bins are temporarily unavailable. Quarantine status is applied when contents are under quality hold per QM procedures. Maintenance status indicates the bin is undergoing physical inspection or repair.

Classification of the physical bin configuration. Fixed bins are dedicated to specific materials (e.g., hazardous chemicals); random bins accept any compatible material; bulk bins hold large quantities; tank bins represent liquid storage vessels in tank farms; floor bins are ground-level pallet positions; rack bins are elevated rack locations.

Type of secondary containment infrastructure present at this storage bin to prevent chemical spills from reaching drains or the environment. Secondary bund and tank bund are used for bulk liquid chemical storage; spill pallets for drum storage; lined floor for general chemical areas. Required for EPA SPCC compliance and ISO 14001 environmental management.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 112 (SPCC); ISO 14001; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119

description: Timestamp when the storage bin master record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for bin lifecycle management and supports data governance requirements.
Current number of handling units (pallets, drums, IBCs) physically present in the bin. Reflects the real-time stock count as maintained by SAP EWM goods movements.
Current total volume in cubic meters occupied by stock in the bin. Compared against max_volume_m3 to determine available capacity for putaway decisions.

Current total gross weight in kilograms of all stock currently stored in the bin. Updated upon each goods movement. Used to monitor bin load against maximum weight capacity and support structural safety compliance.

Date on which the storage bin was or is scheduled to be deactivated and removed from active warehouse operations. Null for currently active bins. Supports bin lifecycle management and warehouse restructuring projects.

NFPA fire protection zone or compartment designation assigned to the storage bin. Used to enforce fire load limits, sprinkler system coverage mapping, and emergency response planning for flammable and combustible chemical storage areas.

Indicates whether this bin is a fixed bin permanently assigned to a specific material or material group, as opposed to a random bin available for any compatible material. True = fixed bin assignment; False = random bin. Drives SAP EWM putaway strategy determination.

Indicates whether this storage bin has been formally approved and certified for the storage of hazardous materials in compliance with OSHA PSM, EPA RMP, and GHS regulations. True = approved for hazmat storage; False = not approved.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119; EPA 40 CFR Part 68; GHS

description: Indicates whether this bin is designated as a quarantine storage location for materials under quality hold, out-of-specification (OOS) status, or pending Certificate of Analysis (COA) release. True = quarantine bin; False = standard bin. Integrates with SAP QM quality inspection workflows.

Date of the most recent physical safety or structural inspection of the storage bin, including rack integrity checks, containment integrity, and hazmat compliance verification. Supports OSHA PSM and ISO 45001 occupational health and safety compliance.
Timestamp of the most recent modification to the storage bin master record. Used for change tracking, data lineage, and audit trail purposes in compliance with ISO 9001 document control requirements.

Date on which the most recent physical inventory count or cycle count was performed for this storage bin. Used to track inventory accuracy compliance, schedule future counts, and support SOX financial reporting requirements for inventory valuation.
Maximum number of handling units (pallets, drums, IBCs, or containers) that can be stored in the bin simultaneously. Enforced during directed putaway to prevent overfilling.

Maximum allowable stacking height in meters for materials stored in this bin. Enforced to prevent structural overload and comply with OSHA stacking safety regulations. Particularly relevant for drum and IBC storage of liquid chemicals.

Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this bin. Prevents thermal degradation, polymerization, or hazardous decomposition of stored chemicals. Critical for compliance with SDS storage requirements.
Maximum storage volume in cubic meters available within the bin. Used for volumetric capacity planning, putaway feasibility checks, and tank farm liquid volume management.

Maximum allowable gross weight in kilograms that the storage bin can safely hold, including packaging and handling units. Critical for structural safety compliance and overload prevention in chemical warehouses storing dense raw materials or bulk liquids.

Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this bin. Enforced for temperature-sensitive chemical raw materials, intermediates, and finished goods to prevent degradation, crystallization, or phase separation.

Scheduled date for the next mandatory safety or structural inspection of the storage bin. Drives preventive maintenance scheduling and ensures continuous compliance with OSHA, NFPA, and ISO 45001 inspection frequency requirements.

Real-time occupancy state of the storage bin indicating whether the bin is empty, fully occupied, partially occupied, or reserved for an incoming transfer order. Drives directed putaway logic in SAP EWM and supports inventory accuracy reporting.

SAP EWM picking point bin code associated with this storage bin. For bulk or reserve storage bins, identifies the forward pick face bin from which order picking is executed. Supports two-step picking workflows common in chemical distribution operations.

SAP EWM putaway strategy assigned to this bin that determines how incoming stock is directed to storage locations. Fixed strategy assigns stock to pre-defined bins; open storage allows any compatible bin; bulk strategy consolidates large quantities; near-picking places fast-movers close to pick areas; addition-to-existing consolidates partial bins.

Inventory lot rotation strategy applied to this bin for picking sequence determination. FIFO (First In First Out) is standard for most chemicals; FEFO (First Expired First Out) is mandatory for materials with expiration dates to prevent use of expired lots; LIFO and LEFO are used in specific bulk storage scenarios. Aligns with GMP and ISO 9001 inventory management requirements.
Row number within the aisle that identifies the horizontal position of the storage bin. Combined with aisle and level, provides the full physical coordinate of the bin within the warehouse.

Vertical level or tier of the storage bin within a rack structure. Level 1 is ground floor; higher numbers indicate elevated rack positions. Used for equipment assignment (e.g., forklift vs. reach truck) and weight distribution planning.

SAP EWM storage section (Lgber) code that subdivides a storage type into logical groupings, such as fast-moving, slow-moving, or temperature-controlled sections. Used to optimize putaway strategies for chemical raw materials and finished goods.

SAP EWM storage type (Lgtyp) code that classifies the physical area of the warehouse where this bin resides, such as high-rack storage, bulk storage, tank farm, or hazardous material area. Drives putaway and picking strategies.

Temperature-controlled storage zone classification for the bin. Ambient bins operate at room temperature; refrigerated bins maintain 2–8°C for temperature-sensitive chemicals or intermediates; heated bins prevent solidification of high-melting-point materials; controlled bins maintain a specific validated temperature range per product specification.

Indicates whether this storage bin requires dedicated ventilation or is located in a ventilated area to manage volatile organic compound (VOC) emissions or hazardous air pollutant (HAP) concentrations. True = ventilation required; False = no special ventilation needed. Supports EHS compliance with EPA and OSHA air quality standards.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 63; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.94; TSCA

description: The measured liquid level height in the tank expressed in meters, as reported by the level transmitter instrument and captured in the Aveva PI System historian. This is the primary raw measurement value for continuous inventory tracking.

description: The current tank fill level expressed as a percentage of the tank's maximum working capacity (0-100%). Provides an intuitive operational view of tank utilization for dashboards, alerts, and capacity planning without requiring knowledge of tank dimensions.
reference: API 650; SAP EWM - Storage Bin Capacity; Aveva PI System

description: Indicates whether the low-level alarm setpoint was active (True) or inactive (False) at the time of measurement. A low-level alarm signals that the tank level has dropped to or below the low alarm threshold, indicating potential supply shortage or pump cavitation risk.
reference: IEC 61511 - Functional Safety; Honeywell Experion DCS - Alarm Management; ISA-18.2

description: The calculated mass of the liquid inventory in the tank expressed in kilograms, derived from volume and density. Used for material balance, production accounting, and regulatory emission calculations (e.g., EPA TRI, VOC reporting).
reference: EPA TRI Reporting; OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119; API MPMS Chapter 11

description: The data quality indicator for this measurement record as reported by the Aveva PI System historian (e.g., Good, Bad, Uncertain, Substituted). Reflects instrument health, communication status, or manual data substitution. Used to filter unreliable readings in analytics and reporting.
reference: Aveva PI System - Data Quality Codes; OPC UA Part 8 - Data Access Quality; ISA-88

description: Identifies the originating source system or method for this level measurement record. Distinguishes between automated PI historian readings, manual gauge readings entered by operators, DCS/SCADA system feeds, calculated values from strapping tables, or estimated values.
reference: Aveva PI System; Honeywell Experion DCS; Siemens SIMATIC PCS 7; ISA-88

description: The precise date and time at which the tank level measurement was recorded by the process historian or instrumentation system. This is the principal real-world event time for this observation record.
reference: Aveva PI System - Tag Timestamp; ISA-88 Batch Control Standard

description: The Aveva PI System historian tag identifier (e.g., SITE01.TK-101.LEVEL) that is the source of this measurement record. Enables traceability back to the originating instrument data stream in the process historian.
reference: Aveva PI System - Tag Configuration; ISA-88

description: The date and time when this tank farm level record was first created or ingested into the Silver Layer data lakehouse. Distinct from measurement_timestamp (the real-world event time). Used for data pipeline auditing, latency monitoring, and record lineage tracking.
reference: ISO 9001 - Record Management; Databricks Lakehouse - Silver Layer Audit

description: The date and time when this tank farm level record was last modified in the Silver Layer, for example due to a data quality correction, manual override, or reprocessing event. Supports change tracking and data lineage auditing.
reference: ISO 9001 - Record Management; Databricks Lakehouse - Silver Layer Audit

description: The inventory mass threshold in kilograms at which a replenishment order should be triggered for this tank. When mass_kg falls at or below this value, procurement or supply chain teams are alerted to initiate a purchase order or transfer. Supports continuous inventory management for bulk chemicals.
reference: SAP MM - MRP Reorder Point Planning; SAP EWM - Replenishment Management

description: The maximum allowable storage temperature for the chemical in this tank, expressed in degrees Celsius, as specified in the material SDS (Safety Data Sheet). Exceeding this threshold may indicate a safety hazard, product degradation risk, or regulatory non-compliance.
reference: GHS - Safety Data Sheet Section 7; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 PSM; NFPA 30 - Flammable Liquids Code

description: The minimum allowable storage temperature for the chemical in this tank, expressed in degrees Celsius, as specified in the material SDS (Safety Data Sheet). Used to trigger temperature deviation alerts and ensure regulatory compliance with safe storage requirements.
reference: GHS - Safety Data Sheet Section 7; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 PSM; NFPA 30 - Flammable Liquids Code

description: The nominal or maximum working capacity of the tank in cubic meters as defined in the tank design specification and strapping table. Stored with the measurement record to enable percentage calculations and capacity utilization reporting without requiring a join to the tank master.
reference: API 650 - Welded Tanks for Oil Storage; ISO 7507 - Tank Calibration; SAP PM - Equipment Master

description: The name or designation of the tank farm area or storage cluster where the tank is physically located (e.g., North Tank Farm, Solvent Storage Area). Supports area-level inventory aggregation and reporting.
reference: SAP EWM - Warehouse Structure; EPA RMP - Facility Siting

description: The temperature of the liquid in the tank at the time of measurement, expressed in degrees Celsius. Temperature is required for density correction, volume-to-mass conversion, and monitoring of temperature-sensitive chemicals to ensure safe storage conditions.
reference: Aveva PI System - Temperature Tag; API MPMS Chapter 11.1 - Temperature Correction; OSHA PSM

description: Indicates whether the measured temperature at the time of this record was outside the defined safe storage temperature range (True = deviation detected, False = within range). Supports EHS compliance monitoring and automated alerting for temperature-sensitive chemical storage.
reference: OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119; GHS SDS Section 7; Intelex EHS Management

description: The available headspace (ullage) in the tank expressed in meters, calculated as the difference between the maximum allowable working level and the current measured level. Critical for safe filling operations, overfill prevention, and scheduling inbound deliveries.
reference: API 650 - Welded Tanks for Oil Storage; OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119; EPA RMP 40 CFR Part 68

description: The available headspace volume in the tank expressed in cubic meters, representing the remaining capacity available for additional material. Used for logistics planning, delivery scheduling, and overfill risk management.
reference: API 650; EPA RMP 40 CFR Part 68; SAP EWM - Capacity Management

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the inventory value is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR). Supports multi-currency reporting for global operations.
reference: ISO 4217 - Currency Codes; FASB ASC 830 - Foreign Currency; SAP FI/CO

description: The calculated volume of liquid in the tank expressed in liters, derived from the level measurement and tank strapping table. Used for inventory quantity reporting and material balance calculations.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 2 - Tank Calibration; SAP MM - Inventory Management

description: The calculated volume of liquid in the tank expressed in cubic meters (m³). Provides SI unit representation for regulatory reporting, engineering calculations, and international trade documentation.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 2; ISO 7507 - Calibration of Vertical Cylindrical Tanks; EPA Tier II Reporting

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the warehouse transfer order record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Assigns inter‑warehouse transfer orders to cost centers for budgeting and expense tracking.

Foreign key linking to order.line_item. Business justification: Inter‑plant transfer orders are often created to fulfill a particular sales order line; linking enables the Transfer Fulfillment tracking report.

Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: transfer_order moves a specific lot from one bin/location to another. The lot_number STRING column is denormalized; adding lot_id FK to inventory.lot normalizes this. One lot can have multiple transfe
Reference to the material (raw material, intermediate, finished good, or packaging) being physically moved by this transfer order. Aligns with SAP MM material master.

Foreign key linking to planning.production_schedule. Business justification: Transfer orders in chemical plants are generated to stage raw materials and intermediates to production areas per the production schedule. This link enables schedule-driven warehouse execution reporti

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Inter-segment cost allocation: transfer orders crossing profit center boundaries require profit center assignment for intercompany/inter-segment accounting. Chemical manufacturing segment reporting re

Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_bin. Business justification: transfer_order has source_bin (STRING) representing the origin bin. Adding source_storage_bin_id FK to inventory.storage_bin normalizes the source bin reference, enabling bin-level capacity tracking a
Reference to the warehouse complex within which this transfer order is executed. Scopes the source and destination bins to a single warehouse facility.
Date and time when the operator physically began executing the transfer order (e.g., first scan of source bin or handling unit). Enables measurement of queue wait time and operator response time.

Free-text or coded reason for cancellation of the transfer order (e.g., material not found at source bin, bin blocked, production order cancelled, duplicate order). Supports root cause analysis and process improvement.
Date and time when the transfer order was cancelled in SAP EWM. Populated only when transfer_order_status = 'cancelled'. Used for exception reporting and warehouse efficiency analysis.

Method used by the operator or system to confirm execution of the transfer order. 'rf_scan' = RF barcode/RFID scanner; 'manual' = paper-based or keyboard entry; 'automated_conveyor' = conveyor system auto-confirmation; 'voice_picking' = voice-directed warehouse system; 'agv' = automated guided vehicle.

Actual quantity confirmed as physically moved by the operator upon transfer order completion. May differ from transfer_quantity due to partial picks, short picks, or discrepancies discovered during execution.
Date and time when the transfer order was fully confirmed in SAP EWM, triggering the inventory goods movement posting. Represents the principal business event time for this transaction.
reference: SAP EWM Transfer Order Confirmation; SAP MM Goods Movement

description: Date and time when the transfer order was first created in SAP EWM. Represents the record audit creation time and the start of the transfer order lifecycle.
reference: SAP EWM Transfer Order Header; ISO 8601

description: Alphanumeric identifier of the specific storage bin, rack location, or tank to which the material is being moved. Represents the physical destination within the warehouse complex.
SAP EWM storage section within the destination storage type, providing logical grouping of target bins.

SAP EWM storage type code for the target location. Used to validate compatibility with material hazard classification and storage requirements (e.g., flammable, corrosive, oxidizer segregation per OSHA PSM and NFPA codes).
reference: SAP EWM Storage Type; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106; NFPA 30

description: Expiration or shelf-life expiry date of the material lot being transferred. Used to enforce FEFO (First Expired First Out) rotation rules and prevent movement of expired material to active storage. Critical for GMP compliance.
reference: GMP 21 CFR Part 211; SAP QM Batch Classification; ISO 9001

description: SAP MM material document number generated upon posting of the inventory goods movement triggered by transfer order confirmation. Links the physical warehouse movement to the financial inventory posting in SAP FI/CO.
reference: SAP MM Material Document; SAP FI/CO Inventory Valuation

description: Total gross weight in kilograms of the material quantity being transferred, including packaging (drums, IBCs, pallets). Used for load planning, equipment capacity validation, and DOT hazmat transport documentation.
reference: DOT 49 CFR; SAP MM Material Master Weight

description: GHS/DOT hazard classification of the material being transferred (e.g., Flammable Liquid Class 3, Corrosive, Oxidizer, Toxic). Drives storage segregation rules and emergency response procedures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flammable_liquid|corrosive|oxidizer|toxic|explosive|compressed_gas|environmental_hazard|none — promote to reference product]
reference: GHS (Globally Harmonized System); DOT 49 CFR 172; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200

description: Indicates whether the material being transferred is classified as hazardous under DOT, OSHA, or GHS regulations. When True, additional handling protocols, PPE requirements, and segregation rules apply. Supports EHS compliance reporting.
reference: DOT 49 CFR; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200; GHS; TSCA

description: Date and time of the most recent modification to the transfer order record in SAP EWM (e.g., quantity change, operator reassignment, status update). Supports audit trail and change tracking.
reference: SAP EWM Transfer Order Header; ISO 8601

description: SAP EWM/WM movement type code (e.g., 311, 312, 321) that drives the inventory posting logic and account determination in SAP FI/CO. Provides granular classification beyond the high-level transfer_type.
Scheduled date and time when the physical movement is planned to begin. Used for warehouse resource scheduling, shift planning, and SLA monitoring against production order requirements.

Numeric priority level assigned to the transfer order for warehouse queue management. Lower values indicate higher urgency (e.g., 1 = urgent production replenishment, 10 = routine reorganization). Used by SAP EWM task interleaving and resource optimization.

Indicates whether the material lot being transferred is under quarantine status (e.g., pending QC release, OOS investigation, CAPA hold). When True, the transfer may only move material to a designated quarantine storage area. Aligns with SAP QM usage decision.
reference: SAP QM Usage Decision; GMP 21 CFR Part 211; ISO 9001

description: Number of the upstream triggering document that initiated this transfer order (e.g., production order number, delivery note, stock transport order, replenishment request). Provides end-to-end traceability from warehouse movement back to business process.
Type of the upstream document that triggered this transfer order. Enables classification of warehouse movements by business process origin for analytics and KPI reporting.

Date by which the material lot must be retested by the QC laboratory before continued use. Distinct from expiration_date; a lot may be within shelf life but require periodic requalification per LIMS-managed testing schedules.
reference: LabWare LIMS; GMP 21 CFR Part 211; ISO 9001

description: SAP EWM storage section within the source storage type, providing a logical grouping of bins (e.g., flammable liquids section, ambient section, cold storage section).
SAP EWM storage type code for the origin location (e.g., bulk storage, high-rack, tank farm, hazardous material cage, quarantine area). Determines handling rules and equipment requirements.

Operational system of record from which this transfer order record was extracted. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer and distinguishes between SAP EWM (Extended Warehouse Management), legacy SAP WM, or manually created records.

SAP MM special stock type indicator identifying ownership or allocation of the material (e.g., consignment stock, project stock, sales-order-specific stock). Affects inventory valuation and availability checks.

SAP EWM/MM stock category of the material being transferred. Determines whether the stock is available for consumption or shipment. 'unrestricted' = freely available; 'quality_inspection' = pending QC release; 'blocked' = not available for use; 'returns' = returned goods; 'restricted' = conditionally available.

Required storage condition code for the material (e.g., ambient, refrigerated, frozen, temperature-controlled, humidity-controlled, inert atmosphere). Validates that the destination bin meets material storage requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ambient|refrigerated|frozen|temperature_controlled|humidity_controlled|inert_atmosphere|flammable_cabinet — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP MM Storage Conditions; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106; NFPA 30

description: Externally visible alphanumeric transfer order number assigned by SAP EWM. This is the business-facing identifier used in warehouse operations, printed on pick/put-away documents, and referenced in MES and WMS communications.

Current lifecycle state of the transfer order within SAP EWM. 'created' = issued but not yet started; 'in_progress' = operator has begun movement; 'partially_confirmed' = partial quantity confirmed; 'confirmed' = fully executed and goods movement posted; 'cancelled' = voided before execution.
Planned quantity of material to be moved as specified on the transfer order at creation time. Expressed in the base unit of measure of the material (e.g., KG, L, EA, MT).

Classification of the warehouse movement purpose. 'replenishment' = restocking a pick face from reserve storage; 'putaway' = placing newly received goods into a bin; 'pick' = retrieving goods for outbound delivery; 'internal_transfer' = bin-to-bin move for reorganization or hazmat segregation; 'return_to_stock' = returning goods from staging or production back to storage.

Base unit of measure for the transfer and confirmed quantities (e.g., KG = kilograms, L = litres, MT = metric tonnes, EA = each, M3 = cubic metres). Aligns with SAP MM material master UOM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|L|EA|MT|G|M3|LB|GAL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP MM Unit of Measure; ISO 80000-1

description: Primary key for warehouse_location

Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: In SAP-based chemical plants, warehouse storage locations map directly to maintenance functional locations (FLOCs) for PSM compliance reporting, hazmat area inspection scheduling, and maintenance plan

Foreign key linking to inventory.hazmat_storage_rule. Business justification: warehouse_location has hazmat-related attributes (ghs_hazard_class_compatibility, fire_zone_code, ventilation_class, nfpa ratings) that should be governed by a hazmat_storage_rule. Adding hazmat_stora
Alphanumeric identifier for the aisle within the warehouse where this storage location is physically situated. Used for warehouse navigation, pick path optimization, and physical inventory counting.
Physical floor area of this storage location in square meters. Used for capacity planning, fire load calculations, and regulatory reporting of storage area dimensions per EPA and OSHA requirements.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 264; NFPA 30; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106

description: Timestamp when this warehouse location master record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management.
reference: SAP EWM Audit Log; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 (Documented Information)

description: NEC/NFPA 70 electrical area classification for this storage location, indicating the presence and likelihood of flammable vapors or combustible dust. Determines requirements for explosion-proof electrical equipment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: unclassified|class_i_div1|class_i_div2|class_ii_div1|class_ii_div2|zone_0|zone_1|zone_2 — promote to reference product]

Applicable EPA regulatory citation(s) governing storage requirements at this location (e.g., '40 CFR Part 264 - Hazardous Waste Storage', '40 CFR Part 68 - RMP'). Used for environmental compliance documentation, permit management, and regulatory audit support.

Identifier for the fire protection zone in which this storage location resides. Fire zones define the boundaries of fire suppression systems (sprinkler zones, deluge systems), fire detection circuits, and emergency evacuation areas. Referenced in PSM and HAZOP documentation.
reference: NFPA 13; NFPA 30; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 (PSM); EPA RMP

description: Maximum allowable floor load in kilograms per square meter for this storage location. Ensures structural integrity of the warehouse floor when storing heavy drums, IBCs, or bulk containers. Referenced in facility engineering and OSHA compliance inspections.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.22; NFPA 1; Building Structural Engineering Standards

business_glossary_term: GHS (Globally Harmonized System) Hazard Class Compatibility
description: Pipe-delimited list of GHS hazard classes that are approved for storage at this location (e.g., 'Flammable Liquid|Oxidizer|Corrosive'). Governs which chemical materials may be placed here based on GHS classification. Used to enforce chemical segregation rules and prevent incompatible co-storage.
reference: GHS (Globally Harmonized System) Rev 9; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

description: Clear storage height in meters for this location. Used to determine maximum pallet stack height, forklift clearance requirements, and sprinkler system design (in-rack vs. ceiling sprinklers per NFPA 13).

business_glossary_term: Incompatible GHS (Globally Harmonized System) Hazard Classes
description: Pipe-delimited list of GHS hazard classes that must NOT be co-stored at this location due to chemical incompatibility or regulatory segregation requirements (e.g., 'Oxidizer|Explosive'). Enforces NFPA and OSHA chemical segregation rules to prevent dangerous reactions.
reference: GHS Rev 9; NFPA 400; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106; EPA 40 CFR Part 264

description: Indicates whether this storage location is approved and certified for the storage of hazardous materials per OSHA, EPA, and DOT regulations. True = approved for hazmat storage; False = not approved. Drives putaway rule enforcement in SAP EWM.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106; DOT 49 CFR; EPA 40 CFR Part 264

description: Indicates whether this storage location is designated as a quarantine zone for materials under quality hold, regulatory hold, or investigation. True = quarantine zone; False = standard storage. Materials in quarantine zones are blocked from use in production or shipment per GMP and ISO 9001 requirements.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; GMP 21 CFR Part 211; SAP QM Quarantine Stock

description: Indicates whether this storage location has active temperature control (refrigeration, heating, or climate control). True = temperature controlled; False = ambient storage. Drives material assignment rules for temperature-sensitive chemicals and cold chain compliance.

Date of the most recent physical inspection or safety audit conducted at this storage location. Used to track inspection frequency compliance per OSHA PSM, EPA RMP, and internal EHS management requirements. Triggers re-inspection workflows when overdue.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 (PSM); EPA 40 CFR Part 68 (RMP); ISO 45001; Intelex EHS Management

description: Timestamp when this warehouse location master record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking, data synchronization with SAP EWM, and audit trail compliance.
reference: SAP EWM Audit Log; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5; SOX

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the storage location within the warehouse network. Corresponds to SAP EWM storage bin or storage section code used in warehouse operations, goods movements, and transfer orders.
reference: SAP EWM Storage Bin Master; ISO 3166

description: Human-readable descriptive name for the storage location (e.g., 'Tank Farm A - Bay 3', 'Hazmat Drum Storage Zone 2', 'Cold Storage Room 1'). Used in operational reports, safety documentation, and warehouse management interfaces.

Current operational lifecycle status of the storage location. 'quarantine' indicates the location is reserved for materials under quality hold. 'blocked' indicates the location is temporarily unavailable for putaway or picking. 'under_maintenance' indicates the location is undergoing inspection or repair.
reference: SAP EWM Storage Bin Status; ISO 9001

description: Classification of the physical storage location type within the warehouse network. Determines applicable storage rules, capacity constraints, and regulatory requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bulk_tank|drum_storage|cold_storage|hazmat_zone|quarantine_bay|finished_goods|intermediate_storage|open_yard — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP EWM Storage Type Configuration; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106

description: Maximum allowable relative humidity percentage for this storage location. Prevents moisture-induced degradation, corrosion, or microbial growth in stored materials. Expressed as a percentage (0–100).

Maximum quantity in kilograms of hazardous material permitted in this storage zone per EPA, OSHA PSM, and NFPA regulations. Used to enforce Tier II reporting thresholds, RMP covered quantity limits, and NFPA maximum allowable quantities (MAQ) per control area.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 68 (RMP); OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 (PSM); NFPA 400; EPCRA Section 312 Tier II

description: Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this location. Prevents thermal degradation, spontaneous reaction, or ignition of stored chemicals. Enforced against material SDS storage condition requirements.
reference: GHS Section 7 Storage Conditions; NFPA 30; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106

description: Maximum volumetric capacity in liters for this storage location. Particularly relevant for bulk tank farms and liquid chemical storage areas. Used to enforce maximum quantity limits per EPA and OSHA regulations.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 264; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106; NFPA 30

description: Maximum allowable weight in kilograms that this storage location can safely hold. Enforced during putaway to prevent structural overloading. Critical for rack integrity compliance and OSHA load rating requirements.
Minimum allowable relative humidity percentage for this storage location. Relevant for hygroscopic chemicals, moisture-sensitive materials, and packaging integrity. Expressed as a percentage (0–100).

Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this location. Enforces cold chain and temperature-sensitive chemical storage requirements. Materials requiring higher minimum temperatures must not be stored here.
reference: GHS Section 7 Storage Conditions; ISO 9001; FDA 21 CFR

description: Scheduled date by which the next physical inspection or safety audit of this storage location must be completed. Drives EHS compliance calendar and corrective action workflows in Intelex EHS Management.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 (PSM); EPA 40 CFR Part 68 (RMP); ISO 45001; Intelex EHS Management

business_glossary_term: NFPA (National Fire Protection Association) Fire Diamond Flammability Rating
description: NFPA 704 flammability hazard rating (red diamond) for the storage location. Scale: 0 (will not burn) to 4 (extremely flammable). Drives fire suppression system design, electrical classification, and hot work permit requirements.

business_glossary_term: NFPA (National Fire Protection Association) Fire Diamond Health Rating
description: NFPA 704 health hazard rating (blue diamond) for the storage location, indicating the degree of health hazard posed by materials stored here. Scale: 0 (no hazard) to 4 (deadly). Posted on location signage for emergency responder awareness.
reference: NFPA 704 Standard System for Identification of the Hazards of Materials for Emergency Response

business_glossary_term: NFPA (National Fire Protection Association) Fire Diamond Reactivity Rating
description: NFPA 704 instability/reactivity hazard rating (yellow diamond) for the storage location. Scale: 0 (stable) to 4 (may detonate). Informs emergency response procedures and incompatible material segregation requirements.

business_glossary_term: NFPA (National Fire Protection Association) Fire Diamond Special Hazard
description: NFPA 704 special hazard symbol (white diamond) for the storage location. Common values: 'W' (water reactive), 'OX' (oxidizer), 'SA' (simple asphyxiant). Empty if no special hazard applies. Used in emergency response planning and SDS alignment.

business_glossary_term: OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration) Regulatory Citation
description: Applicable OSHA regulatory citation(s) governing safety requirements at this storage location (e.g., '29 CFR 1910.106 - Flammable Liquids', '29 CFR 1910.119 - PSM'). Used for workplace safety compliance documentation and OSHA inspection readiness.

SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing or distribution facility to which this warehouse location belongs. Aligns with SAP MM/EWM plant-warehouse structure for inventory valuation and materials management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Plant Master; SAP EWM Warehouse Number

description: Alphanumeric identifier for the rack or bay within the aisle where this storage location is physically situated. Used in conjunction with aisle and level to precisely identify the bin coordinate.

Inventory lot rotation strategy enforced at this storage location. FIFO (First In First Out) is standard for most materials; FEFO (First Expired First Out) is required for materials with expiration dates to prevent use of expired stock. Drives SAP EWM picking strategy configuration.
reference: SAP EWM Picking Strategy; GMP 21 CFR Part 211; ISO 9001

description: SAP MM/EWM storage condition code assigned to this location, used to match materials requiring specific storage conditions (e.g., flammable, corrosive, refrigerated, dry). Enforces material-location compatibility during putaway strategy execution.

description: SAP EWM storage section code grouping this location within the warehouse. Storage sections define areas with similar storage conditions (e.g., flammable liquids section, refrigerated section) and drive putaway and picking strategies.

Classification of the ventilation system installed at this storage location. Determines suitability for storing volatile organic compounds (VOCs), flammable vapors, and hazardous air pollutants (HAPs). Explosion-proof ventilation is required for Class I flammable liquid storage per NFPA 30.
reference: NFPA 30; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.94; EPA 40 CFR Part 63 (HAP); GHS

description: Vertical level or tier identifier within the rack or shelving structure (e.g., '01' for ground level, '02' for second tier). Used for ergonomic storage assignment and weight-bearing capacity management.
SAP EWM warehouse number identifying the logical warehouse complex to which this location belongs. Used for warehouse-level inventory management, transfer order processing, and EWM configuration.
