Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Stock movement must be linked to the lot it moves; replace string lot_number with FK lot_id for traceability.

Foreign key linking to production.manufacturing_order. Business justification: Stock movements (goods issue/receipt) are posted against manufacturing orders in chemical plant ERP systems. This is a fundamental production-inventory integration point for order cost settlement, mat

Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Inventory movement reporting, hazmat movement logs, and regulatory material tracking (TSCA, REACH) require direct material_master reference on each movement record. stock_movement.material_number is a

Foreign key linking to order.outbound_delivery. Business justification: Required for the Delivery Execution report to trace which stock movements belong to each outbound delivery for compliance and audit.

Foreign key linking to planning.planned_order. Business justification: Goods receipts and goods issues in inventory are triggered by planned orders converted to execution documents. Linking stock movements to originating planned orders enables MRP reconciliation, supply
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Supports profit center profitability analysis of inventory movements for internal management reporting.

Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Every stock movement event affects a specific stock position (updating on_hand_quantity, reserved_quantity, etc.). Adding stock_position_id FK to inventory.stock_position creates a direct audit trail
Foreign key linking to inventory.transfer_order. Business justification: Link stock movement to its originating transfer order for end‑to‑end tracking; replace free‑text field with FK.

Foreign key linking to quality.usage_decision. Business justification: Usage decisions in QM directly post stock movements (unrestricted/blocked/scrap). This is the core SAP QM-MM integration in chemical manufacturing. Every quality-driven stock posting must reference th
Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_location. Business justification: Stock movement must reference the source warehouse location entity for accurate location tracking and audit reporting.

Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Core SAP MM/PM integration: chemicals and materials issued from inventory to maintenance work orders (goods issue movement type 261) must reference the consuming work order. This supports maintenance

The exact date and time when the stock movement record was first captured and persisted in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and FIFO/FEFO sequencing validation. Corresponds to SAP CPUDT + CPUTM fields combined.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - CPUDT/CPUTM; SOX; ISO 9001

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the movement_amount valuation (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Corresponds to SAP WAERS field. Required for multi-currency chemical manufacturing operations with global procurement and sales.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO - WAERS; ISO 4217; FASB; IFRS IAS 21

description: The SAP storage location code (Lagerort) to which the material is being moved or received. Represents the target warehouse area, tank farm zone, production staging area, or quarantine location. Null for pure outbound goods issues with no destination location within the plant.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - LGORT (destination); SAP EWM; OSHA PSM

description: The date on the physical or external source document (e.g., vendor delivery note, production order confirmation) that triggered the inventory movement. May differ from posting_date when documents are processed with a lag. Corresponds to SAP BLDAT field.

The expiration or shelf-life end date of the lot/batch being moved. Used for FEFO (First Expired First Out) lot rotation enforcement, quarantine triggering for expired materials, and regulatory compliance. Corresponds to SAP VFDAT (shelf life expiration date) on the batch master. Critical for chemical raw materials, intermediates, and finished goods with defined shelf lives.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - VFDAT; GMP; FDA 21 CFR Part 211; ISO 9001; REACH

description: The result of the FIFO (First In First Out) or FEFO (First Expired First Out) lot rotation validation check performed at the time of goods issue. Passed = correct lot selected per rotation policy; Failed = incorrect lot selected, violating rotation rules; Not Applicable = movement type does not require lot rotation validation (e.g., goods receipt); Overridden = FIFO/FEFO rule was manually overridden with documented justification. Critical for chemical product quality, shelf-life management, and GMP compliance.
reference: SAP EWM; SAP S/4HANA MM; GMP; FDA 21 CFR Part 211; ISO 9001

description: Indicates whether this goods receipt movement was posted to GR blocked stock (Wareneingang gesperrt), meaning the material has been physically received but not yet released for use pending inspection or documentation review. True = posted to blocked GR stock; False = posted directly to unrestricted or inspection stock. Corresponds to SAP movement type 103/105 scenario.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; ISO 9001 Incoming Inspection; GMP

description: Indicates whether the material being moved is classified as a hazardous material requiring special handling, storage, or transport compliance. True = hazardous material subject to EHS regulations (GHS, DOT, OSHA, EPA); False = non-hazardous material. Triggers hazmat storage compliance checks and EHS reporting requirements.
reference: GHS; DOT 49 CFR; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200 HazCom; EPA TSCA; REACH; NFPA

description: The externally-known SAP material document number (Materialbelegs-nummer) assigned by SAP S/4HANA MM upon posting the inventory movement. Serves as the primary business identifier linking back to the source system transaction. Equivalent to MBLNR in SAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MBLNR; SAP EWM

description: The fiscal year in which the material document was posted in SAP S/4HANA MM. Combined with material_document_number forms the unique SAP document key (MBLNR + MJAHR).

The inventory valuation amount (in local currency) associated with this stock movement, representing the financial impact on inventory accounts. Calculated as quantity multiplied by the moving average price or standard price at time of posting. Used for COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) reporting, inventory valuation, and FI/CO integration. Corresponds to SAP DMBTR field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO - DMBTR; FASB ASC 330; SOX; IFRS IAS 2

description: High-level business classification of the inventory movement event. Goods Receipt (GR) covers inbound receipts from vendors or production; Goods Issue (GI) covers consumption or outbound shipments; Stock Transfer covers location-to-location moves; Return covers vendor or customer returns; Scrapping covers material write-offs; Sampling covers quality lab withdrawals; Revaluation covers price or valuation adjustments. Subsumes formerly separate goods_receipt and goods_issue records as movement subtypes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: goods_receipt|goods_issue|stock_transfer|return|scrapping|sampling|revaluation — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; ISO 9001; GMP Guidelines

description: Directional classification of the inventory movement relative to the plant or warehouse: inbound (stock entering the facility, e.g., goods receipt), outbound (stock leaving the facility, e.g., goods issue to customer or production), or internal (stock moving within the facility, e.g., storage location transfer). Used for net stock position calculations and directional reporting.

A coded reason explaining why the inventory movement occurred, beyond what the movement type conveys. Examples include: production consumption, quality rejection, customer return, vendor return, cycle count adjustment, process loss, sampling withdrawal, spillage/waste, regulatory disposal. Corresponds to SAP GRUND field. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: production_consumption|quality_rejection|customer_return|vendor_return|cycle_count_adjustment|process_loss|sampling|spillage|regulatory_disposal — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - GRUND; ISO 9001; EPA TRI Reporting; OSHA

description: Free-text description providing additional context or explanation for the inventory movement reason, supplementing the movement_reason_code. Used for exception documentation, CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) linkage, and audit trail narratives. Corresponds to SAP text fields associated with GRUND.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; ISO 9001 CAPA; GMP

description: Current lifecycle status of the stock movement record. Posted = successfully committed to inventory books; Reversed = cancelled by a subsequent reversal posting; Blocked = movement is on hold pending approval or investigation; Pending Approval = movement requires workflow approval before final posting (e.g., high-value scrapping). Supports workflow management and audit compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; SOX; ISO 9001; GMP

description: SAP movement type code (Bewegungsart) that classifies the inventory transaction. Standard codes include 101 (Goods Receipt for PO), 122 (Return to Vendor), 201 (Goods Issue to Cost Center), 261 (Goods Issue for Production Order), 301 (Transfer Posting), 551 (Scrapping), 601 (Goods Issue for Delivery), 701 (Inventory Difference). Replaces the former movement_type reference table as an embedded code per product design. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 101|122|201|261|301|551|601|701|... — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - BWART; ISO 9001 Inventory Control

description: The unit of measure used in the reference order document (purchase order, sales order, or production order) for this movement. Corresponds to SAP ERFME field. Enables reconciliation between order quantities and inventory quantities when UOM conversion applies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - ERFME; UN/CEFACT Recommendation 20

description: SAP plant code (Werk) identifying the manufacturing or distribution facility where the inventory movement occurred. In chemical manufacturing, plants correspond to production sites, tank farms, or distribution centers. Serves as the primary organizational unit for inventory management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - WERKS; ISO 14001; EPA Facility Reporting

description: The principal business event date on which the inventory movement was officially posted to the books in SAP S/4HANA MM (Buchungsdatum - BUDAT). This is the date used for inventory valuation, financial period assignment, and regulatory reporting. Distinct from the document creation timestamp.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - BUDAT; FASB ASC 330 Inventory; SOX

description: The quantity of material involved in the inventory movement, expressed in the base unit of measure. Positive values represent inbound or internal movements; negative values represent outbound or consumption movements per SAP convention. Supports up to 4 decimal places for precision chemical measurements (e.g., kg, L, mol).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MENGE; ISO 80000 Quantities and Units

business_glossary_term: Quantity in Order Unit of Measure
description: The movement quantity expressed in the order unit of measure (e.g., purchase order UOM or sales order UOM), which may differ from the base unit of measure. For example, a material ordered in drums (DR) but managed in kilograms (KG). Corresponds to SAP ERFMG field.

Indicates whether the material involved in this movement is under quarantine status at the time of the movement. True = material is quarantined (e.g., pending QC release, OOS investigation, regulatory hold, contamination suspicion); False = material is not under quarantine. Supports hazardous material storage compliance and GMP quality hold management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM; LabWare LIMS; GMP; ISO 9001; FDA 21 CFR Part 211; OSHA

description: The line item number within the reference document (e.g., PO line item, production order component line) associated with this inventory movement. Combined with reference_document_number provides precise traceability to the originating document line. Corresponds to SAP EBELP (PO item) field.

The number of the originating reference document (e.g., Purchase Order number, Production Order number, Sales Order number, Delivery number) that triggered this inventory movement. Provides the complete audit trail linking inventory movements back to procurement, production, or sales transactions. Corresponds to SAP EBELN (PO) or AUFNR (order) depending on context.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - EBELN; PP - AUFNR; SD - VBELN; SOX

description: The type of the originating business document that triggered this inventory movement. Purchase Order (PO) for vendor receipts; Production Order or Process Order for manufacturing consumption/receipt; Sales Order for customer-specific stock; Delivery for outbound shipments; Transfer Order for warehouse transfers; Reservation for planned withdrawals. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: purchase_order|production_order|sales_order|delivery|transfer_order|process_order|reservation — promote to reference product]

Indicates whether this stock movement record is a reversal (cancellation) of a previously posted movement. True = this document is a reversal posting that negates a prior movement; False = original movement. Essential for net inventory position calculations and audit trail integrity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - STBLG; SOX; ISO 9001

description: The material document number of the original movement that this record reverses, when reversal_flag is True. Enables bidirectional audit trail linking between original and reversal postings. Corresponds to SAP STBLG field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - STBLG; SOX; GMP

description: The SAP plant code of the sending plant for inter-plant stock transfer movements (STO - Stock Transfer Orders). Populated only for cross-plant transfers. Corresponds to SAP UMWRK field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - UMWRK; SAP EWM

description: SAP special stock indicator (SOBKZ) identifying non-standard stock ownership or custody arrangements. E = Sales Order Stock (make-to-order), K = Consignment Stock at Customer, Q = Project Stock, V = Consignment Stock from Vendor, W = Returnable Transport Packaging. Empty for standard own stock. Critical for chemical toll manufacturing and consignment inventory scenarios.

The SAP stock type category indicating the usability status of the inventory being moved. Unrestricted stock is available for use; Quality Inspection stock is under QC hold pending COA approval; Blocked stock is restricted from use (e.g., OOS - Out of Specification); Restricted stock has conditional use limitations; Returns stock is customer-returned material awaiting disposition. Corresponds to SAP SOBKZ / INSMK fields.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - INSMK; ISO 9001 QMS; GMP; LabWare LIMS

description: The base unit of measure for the movement quantity (e.g., KG for kilograms, L for liters, MT for metric tons, EA for each, DR for drums, IBC for intermediate bulk containers). Corresponds to SAP MEINS (base UOM) field. Critical for chemical inventory accuracy and regulatory reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MEINS; ISO 80000; UN/CEFACT Recommendation 20

description: The date and time when the stock movement record was last modified in the source system or lakehouse silver layer. Supports incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance.

SAP valuation type (Bewertungsart - BWTAR) used when a material is managed with split valuation — for example, differentiating between domestic-sourced and imported raw materials, or between different quality grades of the same chemical. Enables separate inventory valuation for the same material number.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - BWTAR; FASB ASC 330; SOX

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each stock position record representing a material at a specific storage location, bin, or tank farm. Primary key for the stock_position data product.
Primary key for stock_position

Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Needed for daily inventory balance reports linking each stock position to its master chemical product, enabling accurate valuation, SDS lookup, and REACH compliance.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for cost accounting reports that allocate inventory value to cost centers per plant, enabling accurate product cost roll‑up.

Foreign key linking to maintenance.equipment. Business justification: Spare parts allocation for equipment repairs; maintenance work orders consume inventory items per equipment, required for parts consumption reporting.

Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: Stock positions in chemical plants are physically located within process unit functional locations (e.g., reactor area, tank farm). This link supports PSM inventory-by-process-unit reporting, maintena

Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: stock_position tracks a real-time stock position for a specific lot/batch. The lot_number STRING column is denormalized; adding lot_id FK to inventory.lot normalizes this and enables FIFO/FEFO lot rot

Foreign key linking to planning.mrp_run. Business justification: MRP runs consume stock position data (on-hand, reserved, in-transit quantities) as primary inputs to net requirements calculation. Chemical manufacturing planners must trace which MRP run last process
Reference to the material master record identifying the specific raw material, intermediate, finished good, or packaging material held in this stock position. Sourced from SAP MM material master.
Reference to the manufacturing plant or facility where this stock position is held. Aligns with SAP organizational structure for plant-level inventory management.

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Segment profitability reporting (CO-PA): inventory positions are assigned to profit centers for business segment reporting and profitability analysis. Standard SAP controlling requirement in chemical

Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Required for traceability of on‑hand stock to the originating purchase order for regulatory recall and inventory valuation reports.

Foreign key linking to quality.quality_specification. Business justification: Stock positions in chemical manufacturing are managed by quality specification (grade, purity class). This FK enables grade-segregated inventory reporting, ensures correct spec is applied during stock

Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.sds_record. Business justification: OSHA HazCom and GHS require SDS to be accessible at the point of storage for every hazardous chemical. Linking stock_position directly to sds_record enables warehouse safety compliance checks, storage

Foreign key linking to finance.standard_cost. Business justification: Balance sheet inventory valuation: stock_position carries valuation_price and valuation_class used to compute inventory value. Linking to standard_cost enables automated standard cost roll-up, price v

Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_bin. Business justification: stock_position has a storage_bin STRING column representing the bin code. storage_bin is the master record for individual bins within a warehouse location. Adding storage_bin_id FK normalizes the bin
Reference to the specific warehouse storage location, bin, or tank farm where the material is physically stored. Managed within SAP EWM warehouse structure.

Quantity of the material available for unrestricted use, calculated as on-hand quantity minus reserved, blocked, and quality inspection quantities. Drives ATP (Available-to-Promise) checks in SAP SD and production planning in SAP PP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD ATP Logic; ISO 9001

description: Quantity of the material placed in blocked stock status due to quality failures, regulatory holds, damage, or quarantine. Blocked stock is not available for production or shipment until released by QA or EHS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Blocked Stock; GMP; OSHA; REACH

description: The date and time when this stock position record was first created in the source system (SAP EWM). Provides audit trail for record lineage and supports SOX compliance for inventory record integrity.
reference: SAP EWM Audit Trail; SOX Section 404; ISO 9001

description: The date on which the material lot expires and is no longer suitable for use. Critical for FEFO (First Expired First Out) lot rotation logic, regulatory compliance, and preventing use of out-of-specification materials. Sourced from SAP batch classification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Management; GMP 21 CFR Part 211; ISO 9001; REACH

description: The date on which the material was received into the warehouse via a goods receipt posting in SAP MM. Used as the FIFO reference date for lot rotation when manufacture date is unavailable.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Receipt; ISO 9001

description: Indicates whether the material in this stock position is classified as a hazardous material requiring special storage, handling, and reporting under OSHA, DOT, EPA, and REACH regulations. Triggers hazmat storage compliance checks in EHS management.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200 HazCom; DOT 49 CFR; EPA TSCA; REACH; GHS

description: Quantity of the material currently in transit between storage locations, plants, or external suppliers. Represents stock that has been shipped but not yet received at the destination, tracked via SAP EWM transfer orders.
reference: SAP EWM Transfer Orders; Oracle Transportation Management

description: The date on which the most recent physical inventory count or cycle count was performed for this stock position. Used to assess inventory accuracy and schedule next count per SOX and ISO 9001 audit requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Physical Inventory; SOX Section 404; ISO 9001

description: The date of the most recent inventory movement (goods issue, goods receipt, transfer order, or stock transfer) affecting this stock position. Used to identify slow-moving or obsolete inventory for write-off analysis.
reference: SAP EWM Inventory Movement History; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: The inventory rotation strategy applied to this stock position for picking and consumption sequencing. FIFO (First In First Out) is used for most materials; FEFO (First Expired First Out) is mandatory for materials with expiration dates per GMP requirements.
reference: SAP EWM Lot Rotation; GMP 21 CFR Part 211; ISO 9001; FEFO/FIFO Best Practice

description: The date on which the material lot was manufactured or produced. Used in conjunction with expiration_date to calculate shelf life and support FIFO/FEFO rotation. Sourced from SAP batch master or COA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Master; GMP; ISO 9001

description: The maximum allowable stock quantity for this material at this storage location, constrained by tank capacity, regulatory storage limits, or warehouse space. Enforced by SAP EWM capacity checks and EHS hazmat storage quantity limits under OSHA PSM.
reference: SAP EWM Capacity Management; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 PSM; EPA RMP

description: Total physical quantity of the material currently present at the storage location, including all stock types (unrestricted, quality inspection, blocked). Represents the gross inventory position before any reservations or holds are applied.

Quantity of the material currently under quality inspection in the LIMS or SAP QM module. This stock is not available for use until the inspection lot is closed and the material is released to unrestricted stock.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Inspection Lots; LabWare LIMS; ISO 9001; GMP

description: Free-text description of the reason this stock position has been placed in quarantine or blocked status. Examples include failed QC test, regulatory hold, contamination suspicion, or pending CAPA investigation. Populated when stock_status is quarantine or stock_type is blocked.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM; GMP; OSHA; CAPA Process; ISO 9001

description: The minimum stock level at which a replenishment order should be triggered for this material at this storage location. Used by SAP MRP (Material Requirements Planning) to generate purchase requisitions or production orders automatically.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; ISO 9001 Inventory Control

description: Quantity of the material reserved against open production orders, sales orders, or transfer orders in SAP. Reserved stock is committed but not yet physically moved, reducing the available quantity for new demand.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Reservations; SAP PP Production Orders

description: The minimum buffer stock quantity maintained to protect against supply disruptions, demand variability, or lead time uncertainty. Defined in SAP MRP parameters and used in available quantity calculations for production planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP Safety Stock; ISO 9001

description: The date and time at which this stock position record was captured or last refreshed from the source system (SAP EWM/S4HANA). Represents the as-of time for the inventory snapshot, critical for real-time inventory reporting and reconciliation.
reference: SAP EWM Real-Time Inventory; Databricks Silver Layer Data Freshness

description: Indicates whether this stock position represents a special stock type such as consignment inventory, project stock, sales-order-specific stock, or vendor-managed inventory (VMI). Drives distinct accounting and ownership rules in SAP MM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Special Stock; FASB ASC 330

description: Classification of the material type held in this stock position. Drives inventory valuation rules, storage compliance requirements, and reporting segmentation across raw materials, intermediates, finished goods, and packaging materials.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Type; ISO 9001

description: Current lifecycle status of the stock position record. Quarantine status is applied when material is pending investigation or regulatory hold. Expired indicates the material has passed its expiration date. Recalled indicates a product recall event has been triggered.
reference: SAP EWM Stock Status; GMP; OSHA PSM; REACH

description: SAP stock type classification indicating the usability state of the inventory. Unrestricted stock is available for use; quality_inspection stock is pending QC release; blocked stock is not available for use; returns stock is from customer returns; in_transit stock is between locations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Stock Types; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Classification of the physical storage infrastructure type where the material is held. Drives hazardous material storage compliance rules under OSHA PSM, NFPA, and DOT regulations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bulk_tank|drum_rack|pallet_rack|cold_storage|hazmat_cage|silo|ibc_bay — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP EWM Storage Type; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106; NFPA 30; DOT 49 CFR

description: The base unit of measure in which all quantity fields for this stock position are expressed. Chemical manufacturing uses weight (KG, MT), volume (L, GAL, M3), and count (EA, DRUM, IBC) units depending on material type. Aligns with SAP MM base UOM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|LB|L|GAL|MT|TON|EA|BAG|DRUM|IBC|M3|FT3 — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Unit of Measure; ISO 80000 Quantities and Units

description: The date and time when this stock position record was last modified in the source system. Used for change data capture (CDC) processing in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and audit trail maintenance.
reference: SAP EWM Audit Trail; SOX Section 404; Databricks CDC

description: SAP valuation class code that determines the general ledger (GL) accounts to which inventory postings are made for this material. Links inventory movements to the correct COGS and balance sheet accounts per FASB and SOX requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Valuation Class; FASB ASC 330; SOX Section 404

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the valuation_price is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR). Required for multi-currency inventory valuation reporting across global plant locations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; FASB ASC 830

description: Reference unit price used to calculate the inventory valuation of this stock position, expressed in the valuation currency. Carried as a reference attribute for inventory reporting; authoritative valuation is owned by the Finance domain (SAP FI/CO). May reflect standard cost or moving average price per SAP valuation class.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Material Valuation; FASB ASC 330 Inventory; SOX

description: The SAP EWM warehouse number identifying the physical warehouse complex managing this stock position. Used for multi-warehouse inventory segregation and reporting.
Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for the stock reservation record in the inventory management system.
reference: SAP EWM / SAP MM Internal Object Key

description: Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Supports Customer Allocation Report by linking each inventory reservation to the customer account that requested the reservation, enabling order fulfillment tracking.

Foreign key linking to planning.customer_allocation. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing supply constraints, customer allocations authorize specific reservation quantities per customer. Stock reservations must reference the customer allocation that authorized the

Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Project budget consumption monitoring: stock reservations for maintenance shutdowns, R&D, or capital projects are created against internal orders. Linking enables real-time budget consumption tracking

Foreign key linking to product.line_item. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing, stock reservations are created at sales order line item level for lot-controlled and hazmat materials (SAP MRP reservation process). Linking reservation to line_item enables
Reference to the specific inventory lot or batch being earmarked. Supports FIFO/FEFO lot rotation and prevents double-allocation of expiring chemical stock.
reference: SAP MM Batch Management (MCH1); GMP Lot Traceability

description: Foreign key linking to production.manufacturing_order. Business justification: Stock reservations are fundamentally created against manufacturing orders in chemical plant MRP/ERP systems (SAP movement type 261). Direct FK enables manufacturing order material availability confirm
Reference to the material master record for the chemical, raw material, intermediate, finished good, or packaging material being reserved.

Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Needed for allocation reports: reserving stock for a specific sales opportunity ensures availability and supports the Opportunity‑to‑Inventory allocation process.
Reference to the production order, customer delivery order, or maintenance work order that will consume the reserved stock. Establishes the demand source driving the reservation.
reference: SAP PP Production Order / SAP SD Delivery / SAP PM Work Order

description: Foreign key linking to sales.price_agreement. Business justification: Contract-priced reservation valuation: in chemical manufacturing, stock reserved for contract customers must be valued and allocated under the governing price agreement. This link enables accurate res

Foreign key linking to production.process_order. Business justification: Stock reservations in chemical manufacturing are created against process orders to ensure material availability before production starts. Direct FK enables material availability checks and reservation

Foreign key linking to quality.quality_specification. Business justification: Stock reservations for chemical products must specify the required quality specification (grade, purity, regulatory compliance). In chemical manufacturing, customers order to specific quality specs; r

Foreign key linking to sales.quote. Business justification: Quote-driven inventory reservation: when a customer accepts a quote, chemical manufacturers reserve stock against that quote prior to order conversion. This link enables quote-to-reservation traceabil

Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: A stock reservation earmarks a specific quantity from a specific stock position. Adding stock_position_id FK to inventory.stock_position enables direct linkage between reservations and the position be
Reference to the warehouse location or tank farm where the reserved stock is physically stored.
Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Reservation of inventory for a specific maintenance work order; needed for parts reservation and work order cost tracking.

Indicates whether the reservation should be automatically released back to available stock upon reaching the expiry date without fulfillment. Supports automated inventory management and prevents indefinite stock lock-up.
reference: SAP EWM Reservation Management; Chemical Mfg Inventory Policy

description: Indicates whether a Certificate of Analysis must be verified and attached before the reserved stock can be issued to the consuming order. Enforces quality gate compliance for regulated chemical products.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; GMP 21 CFR Part 211; LabWare LIMS COA Workflow

description: Specifies the type of the consuming order document. Distinguishes between production orders, process orders (for batch chemical manufacturing), sales delivery orders, maintenance orders, and project orders to support domain-specific analytics.
reference: SAP PP/SD/PM Order Category; SAP MM Reservation Item Category (RSPOS)

description: The date and time when the stock reservation record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, SLA tracking, and data lineage.
reference: SAP MM Change Document; ISO 8601; SOX Audit Trail Requirements

description: The date after which the reservation is no longer valid and the earmarked stock is automatically released back to available inventory. Critical for preventing indefinite stock lock-up of expiring chemical materials.
reference: SAP MM Reservation Expiry; FEFO Inventory Management; GMP Shelf Life Management

description: Cumulative quantity of the reservation that has already been issued or withdrawn against the consuming order. Used to calculate the remaining open quantity and determine partial fulfillment status.
reference: SAP MM Goods Issue (WAMNG); SAP EWM

description: Indicates whether the reserved material is classified as hazardous. When true, additional handling, storage compliance, and transport regulations apply per OSHA, DOT, and REACH requirements.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200; DOT 49 CFR; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; GHS

description: The date and time when the stock reservation record was most recently modified, such as a quantity change, status update, or date revision. Supports change tracking and data reconciliation.
reference: SAP MM Change Document; ISO 8601; SOX Audit Trail Requirements

description: The ERP material number (SAP material number) of the chemical or material being reserved. Serves as the externally-known business identifier for cross-system reference and reporting.
reference: SAP MM Material Master (MATNR); Chemical Mfg SKU Catalog

description: SAP goods movement type code that will be used when the reservation is fulfilled (e.g., 261 for goods issue to production order, 601 for goods issue to customer delivery). Determines the accounting and inventory impact of the eventual withdrawal.
reference: SAP MM Movement Type (BWART); SAP FI/CO Integration

description: Free-text field for operational notes, special handling instructions, or comments entered by the planner or warehouse operator at the time of reservation creation or update.

description: Indicates whether the reserved lot has been flagged as Out of Specification (OOS) by the quality management system. An OOS reservation is blocked from fulfillment pending CAPA resolution.

description: Quantity of material placed under quarantine hold. Represents the principal quantitative fact of this hold record. Must be expressed in the base unit of measure for the material.

Standardized code indicating the primary reason for the quarantine hold. OOS = Out of Specification; OOT = Out of Trend; CUSTOMER_COMPLAINT = customer-reported quality issue; REGULATORY_HOLD = regulatory agency directive; DAMAGE = physical damage; CONTAMINATION = cross-contamination or foreign material; LABEL_ERROR = incorrect or missing labeling; PENDING_COA = Certificate of Analysis (COA) not yet received. reference: SAP S/4HANA QM; LabWare LIMS; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; GMP 21 CFR Part 211

description: Free-text narrative describing the specific reason for placing the material under quarantine hold. Provides context beyond the hold reason code, including test failure details, complaint summary, or regulatory directive reference.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM; LabWare LIMS; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7

description: Current lifecycle state of the quarantine hold. 'open' indicates active restriction; 'released' indicates disposition approved for use/shipment; 'rejected' indicates material condemned or destroyed; 'pending_review' awaits disposition decision; 'escalated' requires senior QA or regulatory review; 'closed' is administratively finalized.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.7; GMP 21 CFR Part 211

description: Classification of the quarantine hold by originating function: 'quality' for QC/QA-initiated holds; 'regulatory' for holds triggered by EPA, OSHA, or ECHA directives; 'safety' for EHS-initiated holds; 'customer' for customer complaint-driven holds; 'environmental' for environmental compliance holds; 'administrative' for documentation or system-related holds.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM; Intelex EHS; ISO 9001:2015; ISO 14001:2015; OSHA 29 CFR 1910

description: Source system that triggered or originated the quarantine hold (e.g., LIMS = LabWare LIMS for OOS results; EHS = Intelex EHS for safety incidents; SAP_QM = SAP Quality Management for inspection lot failures; MES = Manufacturing Execution System; MANUAL = manually initiated by QA personnel). description: Full name of the person who initiated the quarantine hold. Retained for audit trail and regulatory reporting purposes where employee ID alone is insufficient.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM; GMP 21 CFR Part 211; ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5

Criticality classification of the functional location using the SAP PM ABC indicator. 'A' = critical (production-critical, safety-critical), 'B' = important (significant impact), 'C' = non-critical (minimal impact). Drives maintenance prioritization, spare parts stocking, and OEE improvement focus. Corresponds to SAP PM IFLOT-ABCKZ.
reference: SAP PM IFLOT; ISO 14224; ISO 55001; OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119

description: Financial fixed asset number from SAP FI/CO Asset Accounting linked to this functional location. Enables reconciliation between the maintenance asset hierarchy and the financial asset register for CapEx tracking, depreciation, and SOX compliance.
reference: SAP PM IFLOT-ANLNR; SAP FI/CO; FASB ASC 360; SOX Section 302

description: Physical building, bay, or plant area code where the functional location is situated. Used for maintenance crew routing, emergency response, and facility management. Complements the hierarchical floc_code with a physical location reference.
reference: SAP PM IFLOT; NFPA 1 Fire Code; OSHA 29 CFR 1910

description: SAP PM catalog profile assigned to this functional location, defining the damage codes, cause codes, and activity codes available for maintenance notifications and work orders. Ensures standardized failure coding for reliability analysis and CAPA workflows. Corresponds to SAP PM IFLOT-RBNR.
reference: SAP PM IFLOT; ISO 14224 Failure Taxonomy

description: Date on which the functional location was formally commissioned and handed over to operations. Marks the start of the operational lifecycle and the beginning of preventive maintenance scheduling. May differ from installation_date for complex systems.
reference: SAP PM IFLOT; ISO 55001; OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119

description: SAP company code (Buchungskreis) representing the legal entity that owns this functional location. Required for financial reporting, asset accounting, and SOX compliance. Corresponds to SAP PM IFLOT-BUKRS.
reference: SAP PM IFLOT; SAP FI/CO; SOX Section 302/404; FASB

description: Primary material of construction for the functional location (e.g., 'Carbon Steel A516-70', '316L Stainless Steel', 'Hastelloy C-276', 'FRP'). Critical for corrosion management, inspection planning, and chemical compatibility assessment under REACH and PSM requirements.
reference: OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119; ASME B31.3; ISO 14224; REACH Regulation EC 1907/2006

description: Timestamp when this functional location record was first created in the source system (SAP PM). Provides audit trail for asset master data governance and SOX compliance.
reference: SAP PM IFLOT; SOX Section 302/404; ISO 55001

description: Date on which the functional location was formally decommissioned and removed from active service. Null for active locations. Required for asset lifecycle reporting, regulatory closure documentation, and EPA/OSHA decommissioning notifications.
reference: SAP PM IFLOT; ISO 55001; EPA RCRA; OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119

description: The externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the functional location within the plant hierarchy, as maintained in SAP PM (IFLOT-TPLNR). Follows the plant-unit-area-system-subsystem naming convention (e.g., CH01-RX-001-P-001). This is the business identifier used on work orders, P&IDs, and maintenance records.
reference: SAP PM IFLOT; ISO 14224 Equipment Taxonomy

description: Floor level or elevation reference (e.g., 'Grade Level', 'EL +12.5m', 'Mezzanine Level 2') for the functional location within a building or structure. Supports maintenance crew navigation and emergency response planning.

Broad equipment category grouping for the functional location. Used for maintenance strategy segmentation, PSM mechanical integrity scope definition, and EHS compliance reporting. Aligns with ISO 14224 taxonomy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: process_equipment|utility_equipment|instrumentation|electrical|civil_structural|safety_system|environmental_control — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Human-readable description of the functional location, describing its physical or logical purpose within the plant (e.g., 'Reactor Feed Pump — Unit 3 Ethylene Plant'). Corresponds to SAP PM IFLOT-PLTXT.

GPS latitude coordinate of the functional location in decimal degrees (WGS84). Enables spatial analytics, emergency response planning, and integration with plant GIS systems for asset location visualization.

GPS longitude coordinate of the functional location in decimal degrees (WGS84). Enables spatial analytics, emergency response planning, and integration with plant GIS systems for asset location visualization.

Electrical area classification for the functional location per NEC/IEC standards, indicating the presence and likelihood of flammable gases or vapors. Drives equipment selection, inspection requirements, and EHS compliance. Critical for OSHA and NFPA compliance.
reference: NFPA 70 NEC Article 500; IEC 60079; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.307

description: Inspection methodology classification for this functional location. 'RBI' (Risk-Based Inspection) applies to pressure equipment per API 580/581; 'fixed_interval' for time-based statutory inspections; 'condition_based' for CBM programs; 'run_to_failure' for non-critical assets. Drives inspection planning and regulatory compliance.
reference: API 580/581 Risk-Based Inspection; OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119; ISO 14224; ASME PCC-3

description: Date on which the functional location (equipment or system) was physically installed and commissioned at the plant. Used for asset age calculations, depreciation scheduling, and mechanical integrity baseline establishment.
reference: SAP PM IFLOT; ISO 55001 Asset Management; FASB ASC 360

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this functional location record in the source system (SAP PM). Supports change tracking, data lineage, and master data governance audit requirements.
reference: SAP PM IFLOT; SOX Section 302/404; ISO 55001

description: Date of the most recently completed inspection for this functional location. Used to calculate inspection overdue status, support audit readiness, and demonstrate PSM mechanical integrity compliance to regulators.

Indicates whether this functional location represents a linear asset (e.g., pipeline, cable tray, conveyor) as opposed to a point asset. Linear assets require linear reference measurement for inspection and maintenance location identification.
reference: SAP PM Linear Asset Management; ISO 55001

description: Current operational lifecycle status of the functional location. Drives maintenance scheduling eligibility, work order creation, and PSM mechanical integrity scope. 'Decommissioned' locations are retained for historical traceability.
reference: SAP PM IFLOT; OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119; ISO 55001 Asset Management

description: Hierarchical level classification of the functional location within the plant topology. Defines whether this node represents a plant, process unit, area, system, or sub-system. Drives hierarchy navigation and maintenance planning scope.
reference: SAP PM IFLOT; ISO 14224 Equipment Taxonomy; OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119

description: SAP PM maintenance strategy assigned to this functional location, defining the preventive maintenance cycle (e.g., time-based, condition-based, predictive). Drives automatic generation of maintenance orders and inspection plans. Corresponds to SAP PM IFLOT-MSTRAT.

Indicates whether any modification to this functional location requires a formal Management of Change (MOC) workflow per OSHA PSM requirements. Typically true for PSM-covered locations and safety-critical systems. Drives MOC initiation in the change management process.
reference: OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119(l); ACC Responsible Care; ISO 45001

description: Date of the next scheduled statutory or preventive inspection for this functional location. Used for maintenance planning, regulatory compliance tracking, and PSM mechanical integrity program management. Derived from inspection intervals but stored for operational scheduling.

SAP PM object type code classifying the functional location by equipment category (e.g., reactor, pump, heat exchanger, vessel, compressor, instrumentation). Drives maintenance strategy assignment and spare parts planning. Corresponds to SAP PM IFLOT-EQART. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reactor|pump|heat_exchanger|vessel|compressor|instrumentation|piping|column|tank|filter — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP PM IFLOT; ISO 14224 Equipment Taxonomy

description: Primary process historian tag identifier in the Aveva PI System associated with this functional location. Enables linkage between the SAP PM asset hierarchy and real-time process data for condition-based maintenance, OEE analysis, and predictive analytics.
reference: Aveva PI System; ISA-88 Batch Control; ISA-95 Enterprise-Control Integration

description: Reference number or drawing identifier of the P&ID document on which this functional location appears. Critical for PSM mechanical integrity, MOC workflows, and HAZOP studies. Enables traceability between the physical asset hierarchy and engineering drawings.
reference: OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119; ISA-5.1 P&ID Standards; NFPA 30

description: SAP PM planner group responsible for planning and scheduling maintenance activities at this functional location. Used for workload distribution, capacity planning, and maintenance KPI reporting. Corresponds to SAP PM IFLOT-INGRP.

Maximum allowable working pressure (MAWP) for the functional location in bar units. Used for mechanical integrity assessments, inspection planning, and PSM compliance. Applies primarily to pressure vessels, reactors, and piping systems.
reference: OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119; ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code; ISO 14224

description: Name of the chemical process unit or production unit to which this functional location belongs (e.g., 'Ethylene Cracker Unit 1', 'Sulfuric Acid Plant', 'Polymerization Train B'). Provides operational context for maintenance planning and P&ID alignment.
reference: SAP PM; OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119; P&ID Standards ISA-5.1

description: Indicates whether this functional location is within the scope of OSHA Process Safety Management (PSM) regulations (29 CFR 1910.119). PSM-covered locations require mechanical integrity programs, PHA/HAZOP studies, and MOC documentation.
reference: OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119; ACC Responsible Care

description: Alphanumeric sort field used for grouping and filtering functional locations in maintenance reports and work order lists. Typically encodes plant area, equipment class, or operational unit for reporting convenience. Corresponds to SAP PM IFLOT-EQFNR.
Maximum allowable design temperature for the functional location in degrees Celsius. Used for material selection validation, mechanical integrity assessments, and process safety compliance.
reference: OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119; ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code; ISO 14224

description: SAP work center responsible for performing maintenance activities at this functional location. Used for capacity planning, scheduling, and cost allocation. Corresponds to SAP PM IFLOT-GEWRK.
reference: SAP PM IFLOT; SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center

description: Boolean indicator that a Certificate of Analysis (COA) has been issued for this batch. COA is required for customer shipments and regulatory compliance.
reference: ISO 9001; GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice); COA (Certificate of Analysis)

description: Unique identifier for the Certificate of Analysis issued for this batch. Links the batch record to the quality test results and customer documentation.
Free-text field for operator or supervisor comments, observations, or notes about the batch execution. Used for capturing contextual information not covered by structured fields.
Date and time when this batch record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for electronic records compliance (21 CFR Part 11).

Unique batch identifier from the Distributed Control System (Honeywell Experion, Siemens PCS 7) that executed the process automation. Links the batch record to real-time process data historian (Aveva PI System).
reference: Honeywell Experion DCS; Siemens SIMATIC PCS 7; Aveva PI System

description: Number of process deviations, exceptions, or Out of Specification (OOS) events recorded during batch execution. Triggers CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) and RCI (Root Cause Investigation) workflows.
reference: CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action); GMP; ISO 9001

description: Boolean indicator that one or more exceptions occurred during batch execution. Used to filter batches requiring quality review or management attention.

Date after which the batch is no longer suitable for use or sale. Calculated based on manufacturing date and product shelf-life specification. Critical for FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory management.
reference: GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice); ISO 9001; FEFO Inventory Practice

description: Version identifier of the formula or recipe used for this batch. Critical for traceability when formulas are revised due to MOC (Management of Change) or process optimization.
reference: MOC (Management of Change); ISO 9001; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Boolean indicator that this batch was produced under GMP-compliant conditions and meets all regulatory requirements for pharmaceutical or food-contact chemical production.
reference: GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice); FDA (Food and Drug Administration)

description: Date and time when this batch record was last updated. Part of the audit trail for electronic records compliance and change tracking.
Calendar date of batch production. Used for shelf-life calculation, expiry date determination, and regulatory labeling (GHS - Globally Harmonized System).
Date and time when batch production was completed and the batch was ready for quality inspection. Used for cycle time analysis and production scheduling.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; SAP S/4HANA PP; Honeywell Experion DCS

description: Date and time when batch production was initiated on the manufacturing floor. Captured from MES (Manufacturing Execution System) or DCS (Distributed Control System) transaction log.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; SAP S/4HANA PP; Honeywell Experion DCS

description: Version of the MES or DCS system that recorded this batch. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance and system validation audit trail.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records); MES (Manufacturing Execution System)

description: Unique transaction identifier from the MES system that initiated or recorded this batch. Used for audit trail linkage and electronic records compliance (21 CFR Part 11).
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records); MES (Manufacturing Execution System)

description: Average pH level during the critical reaction phase. Key process parameter for chemical synthesis quality control and product specification compliance.
reference: SPC (Statistical Process Control); ISO 9001; pH (Potential of Hydrogen)

description: Average process pressure during the critical reaction phase, measured in bar. Key process parameter for quality control and process safety management (PSM).
reference: PSM (Process Safety Management); SPC; Honeywell Experion DCS

description: Average process temperature during the critical reaction phase, measured in degrees Celsius. Key process parameter for quality control and SPC (Statistical Process Control) analysis.
reference: SPC (Statistical Process Control); ISO 9001; Honeywell Experion DCS

description: Total duration of the chemical reaction phase in minutes. Critical process parameter for yield optimization and product quality consistency.
Comma-separated list or summary of recipe phases executed during this batch (e.g., charging, heating, reaction, cooling, discharge). Provides high-level execution summary for batch genealogy.
reference: ISA-88 (Batch Control Standard); SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Boolean indicator that this batch is under regulatory hold and cannot be released to inventory or shipped until regulatory clearance is obtained. May be set by EHS (Environment Health and Safety) or quality assurance.
reference: EPA (Environmental Protection Agency); OSHA; EHS (Environment Health and Safety)

description: Date when the batch must be retested to confirm continued compliance with quality specifications. Common for raw materials and intermediate products with extended storage periods.
Current status of the batch record review and approval workflow. Governs whether the batch can be released to inventory or requires additional quality or management review.
reference: GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice); ISO 9001; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Date and time when the batch record was reviewed and approved or rejected by quality assurance. Part of the electronic signature audit trail (21 CFR Part 11).
Calculated yield efficiency as a percentage of actual yield to planned batch size. Key performance indicator for production efficiency and process optimization (SPC - Statistical Process Control).
reference: ISO 22400 (KPI for Manufacturing); SPC (Statistical Process Control)

Indicates whether the customer is currently subject to a credit block in SAP SD (KNKK-CTLPC / KNVV-AUFSD). When True, new sales orders for this customer are blocked from delivery and billing until the block is resolved by the credit department. Critical control for order-to-cash risk management.

description: Reason code explaining why the customer credit block is active. 'overlimit' indicates exposure exceeds approved limit; 'payment_overdue' indicates past-due invoices; 'credit_review' indicates periodic review in progress; 'legal_dispute' indicates an active dispute; 'sanctions_hold' indicates a regulatory/compliance hold; 'manual_hold' indicates a discretionary hold by the credit analyst.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD — Credit Management; OFAC (Office of Foreign Assets Control) — Sanctions Compliance
