description: Foreign key linking to ehs.safety_data_sheet. Business justification: SDS distribution compliance process: a customer's regulatory profile mandates a specific SDS version and language (OSHA HazCom, REACH Article 31). Linking to the actual SDS record replaces the denorma

Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.sds_record. Business justification: Customer SDS Compliance Distribution: regulatory_profile.sds_version_required is a denormalized reference to a specific SDS record. Chemical manufacturers must link each customer's regulatory profile

Comma-separated list of chemical product categories or families that are approved for sale to this customer under the current regulatory profile (e.g., solvents, adhesives, specialty polymers). Restricts order processing to compliant product types.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, TSCA, SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Indicates whether the customer has agreed to grant the supplier audit rights to verify compliance with regulatory requirements, safe handling practices, and end-use declarations for supplied chemicals.
reference: ISO 9001, REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, ACC Responsible Care, SOX

description: Date on which the customer formally agreed to audit rights provisions. Used to track agreement currency and renewal obligations.
reference: ISO 9001, REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, ACC Responsible Care, SOX

description: Indicates whether a Certificate of Analysis must accompany each shipment to this customer, confirming that the chemical product meets specified quality and composition standards.
reference: ISO 9001, GMP, LabWare LIMS, SAP S/4HANA QM

description: Indicates whether a Certificate of Compliance is required with each shipment, attesting that the product conforms to applicable regulatory and customer-specified requirements.
reference: ISO 9001, SAP S/4HANA QM, ACC Responsible Care

description: Timestamp when this regulatory profile record was first created in the system, establishing the audit trail for compliance record-keeping purposes.
reference: ISO 9001, SOX, SAP S/4HANA SD, Salesforce CRM

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary country of destination for chemical product shipments to this customer. Drives applicable regulatory framework selection (e.g., REACH for EU, TSCA for USA).
reference: ISO 3166-1, REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, TSCA, DOT, GHS

description: Geographic region or trade bloc of the customer destination (e.g., European Union, North America, Asia-Pacific) used for regional regulatory framework grouping and reporting.

Indicates whether this customer qualifies as a REACH downstream user and has been notified of their obligations under REACH, including use conditions, exposure scenarios, and risk management measures.
Date from which this regulatory profile becomes binding and governs chemical product sales and documentation obligations for the customer.

business_glossary_term: Environment Health and Safety (EHS) Certification Expiry Date
description: Expiry date of the customer's EHS certification. Triggers re-validation workflow when approaching expiry to ensure continued compliance eligibility.

business_glossary_term: Environment Health and Safety (EHS) Certification Number
description: Certificate number or registration identifier for the customer's EHS management system certification (e.g., ISO 14001 certificate number), used for audit trail and verification.
reference: ISO 14001, ISO 45001, OSHA, Intelex EHS Management

business_glossary_term: Environment Health and Safety (EHS) Certification Status
description: Current status of the customer's EHS certification relevant to handling and using chemical products (e.g., ISO 14001, ISO 45001, OSHA VPP). Determines eligibility for certain product categories.
reference: ISO 14001, ISO 45001, OSHA, Intelex EHS Management, ACC Responsible Care

description: Customer's declared intended end-use application for the chemical products purchased (e.g., industrial solvent, agricultural formulation, polymer additive, pharmaceutical intermediate). Required for REACH downstream user obligations and export control compliance.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, TSCA, ECHA, EAR

description: Industry sector classification of the customer's end-use application for purchased chemicals. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: automotive|construction|electronics|agriculture|pharmaceuticals|consumer_goods|coatings|textiles|food_contact|industrial — promote to reference product]
Date on which this regulatory profile expires and must be renewed or re-validated. Null indicates an open-ended profile with no scheduled expiry.
Export license or authorization number issued by the originating country's regulatory authority for controlled chemical substances, required for dual-use or restricted chemicals.
reference: EAR (Export Administration Regulations), TSCA, DOT, EPA

description: Indicates whether the Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labelling of Chemicals applies to products sold to this customer, requiring GHS-compliant Safety Data Sheets and labels.
reference: GHS (UN Globally Harmonized System), OSHA HazCom 2012, REACH CLP Regulation

description: DOT/IATA/IMDG hazardous materials transport classification applicable to chemical products shipped to this customer (e.g., Class 3 Flammable Liquid, Class 8 Corrosive). Governs packaging, labeling, and shipping documentation requirements.
reference: DOT 49 CFR, IATA DGR, IMDG Code, NFPA, Oracle Transportation Management

description: Customer's import license or permit number issued by the destination country's regulatory authority, required for controlled or restricted chemical substances. Must be validated before shipment.
reference: TSCA, REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, DOT, ECHA, EPA

description: Date of the most recent regulatory compliance audit conducted at or for this customer. Used to schedule next audit and assess compliance currency.
reference: ISO 9001, ISO 14001, REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, Intelex EHS Management

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this regulatory profile record. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and downstream data synchronization.
reference: ISO 9001, SOX, SAP S/4HANA SD, Salesforce CRM

business_glossary_term: Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) Required Flag
description: Indicates whether the legacy MSDS format (pre-GHS) is required in addition to or instead of the current SDS format, applicable for customers in jurisdictions that have not fully adopted GHS.
Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this regulatory profile to ensure continued accuracy, completeness, and alignment with current regulatory requirements.
reference: ISO 9001, REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, TSCA, ACC Responsible Care

description: Externally-known business identifier for this regulatory profile, used in correspondence, audits, and regulatory submissions. Format: REGP-YYYYMMDD-CountryCode.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD, REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, TSCA

description: Current lifecycle state of the customer regulatory profile. Active indicates all requirements are met and sales may proceed; pending_review indicates documentation is under assessment; suspended indicates a compliance hold; expired indicates the profile requires renewal.
Classification of the customer relationship type that determines applicable regulatory obligations and documentation requirements for chemical product sales.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD, REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, TSCA

description: Indicates whether California Proposition 65 (Safe Drinking Water and Toxic Enforcement Act) warning requirements apply to products sold to this customer based on end-use state or distribution territory.

business_glossary_term: Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) Applicable Flag
description: Indicates whether the European Chemicals Agency REACH regulation applies to chemical products sold to this customer, based on destination country and product use.
Email address of the designated regulatory affairs or EHS contact at the customer organization for compliance documentation, SDS distribution, and regulatory notifications.
reference: GDPR Article 4, REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, Salesforce CRM

description: Full name of the designated regulatory affairs or EHS contact person at the customer organization responsible for managing compliance documentation and regulatory correspondence.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, ISO 14001, Salesforce CRM

description: Comma-separated list of chemical product categories that are restricted or prohibited from sale to this customer based on regulatory, end-use, or jurisdictional constraints. Triggers order block in SAP S/4HANA SD.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, TSCA, RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU, SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Date on which the customer formally acknowledged the supplier's Restricted Substance List. Used to track acknowledgment currency and trigger renewal when the RSL is updated.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU, ECHA

description: Indicates whether the customer has formally acknowledged receipt and acceptance of the supplier's Restricted Substance List, confirming awareness of substances that are prohibited or restricted in supplied products.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU, ECHA, ACC Responsible Care

business_glossary_term: Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) Applicable Flag
description: Indicates whether EU RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances) directive requirements apply to chemical products or materials sold to this customer, particularly relevant for electronics-sector customers.
ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) specifying the required language for Safety Data Sheets provided to this customer (e.g., en, de, fr, zh-CN).
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, GHS, OSHA HazCom 2012, ISO 639-1

description: Free-text description of any customer-specific or jurisdiction-specific labeling requirements beyond standard GHS/SDS requirements, such as additional language requirements, customer-specific hazard statements, or country-specific pictogram standards.
reference: GHS, REACH CLP Regulation (EC) No 1272/2008, OSHA HazCom 2012, NFPA 704

business_glossary_term: Substances of Very High Concern (SVHC) Notification Required Flag
description: Indicates whether the customer requires proactive notification when any supplied product contains Substances of Very High Concern (SVHC) above 0.1% w/w threshold, as mandated under REACH Article 33.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 Article 33, ECHA SVHC Candidate List

business_glossary_term: Toxic Substances Control Act (TSCA) Applicable Flag
description: Indicates whether the US EPA Toxic Substances Control Act requirements apply to chemical products sold to this customer.

Primary UN number identifying the hazardous substance or article for transport documentation purposes. Required on shipping papers, labels, and placards for regulated chemical shipments to this customer.
reference: DOT 49 CFR, IATA DGR, IMDG Code, GHS, UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the customer service case or support ticket. Primary key for the case data product.
Reference to the customer account that initiated or is associated with this case.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Object; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Reference to the chemical product involved in this case, particularly relevant for quality complaints, technical inquiries, and documentation requests (SDS, COA, COC).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; SAP QM

description: Reference to the specific customer contact person who submitted or is the primary point of contact for this case.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Customer complaint cases in chemical manufacturing (quality complaints, CAPA investigations, 8D reports) incur investigation and remediation costs tracked to quality or EHS cost centers. Finance requi

Foreign key linking to customer.site. Business justification: Customer service cases in chemical manufacturing are frequently site-specific — a quality complaint, delivery issue, or technical problem occurs at a specific customer plant or facility. The `case` pr

Foreign key linking to maintenance.equipment. Business justification: Customer quality cases (contamination, off-spec batches) are traceable to specific production or filling equipment. Equipment-level case linkage enables quality trend analysis per asset, supports PSM

Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Customer complaint cases in chemical manufacturing reference the specific inspection lot of the shipped batch under dispute. This link enables direct traceability from complaint to the QC inspection r

Foreign key linking to product.line_item. Business justification: Customer complaint cases in chemical manufacturing frequently relate to a specific order line (wrong grade, specification deviation, short-ship on one SKU). A direct FK to line_item enables line-level

Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Customer quality complaints and CAPA investigations in chemical mfg are always tied to a specific production lot for traceability, recall management, and regulatory reporting. The case table currently

Foreign key linking to maintenance.notification. Business justification: Customer complaint cases often originate from or are linked to plant maintenance notifications (equipment failure causing off-spec product, safety incident). This link enables root cause traceability

Foreign key linking to order.order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Order Issue Management report links each service case to the originating sales order for root‑cause analysis and compliance tracking.

Foreign key linking to product.outbound_delivery. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing, customer complaint cases (quality, COA discrepancy, wrong product) are triggered by a specific delivery event. Linking case to outbound_delivery enables CAPA traceability an

Foreign key linking to customer.qualification. Business justification: In chemical manufacturing, quality cases (complaints, out-of-spec events, CAPA investigations) are directly tied to the specific product qualification under which the chemical was approved for use at

Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Customer complaint cases in chemical manufacturing are formally linked to quality deviation records (NCRs). This enables the standard complaint-to-CAPA traceability chain required by ISO 9001 and GMP

Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Customer quality cases (complaints, OOS events) in chemical manufacturing are frequently traced to a specific raw material vendor as root cause. CAPA and 8D reporting processes require linking the cus

Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Customer quality complaint cases in chemical manufacturing trigger corrective maintenance work orders. CAPA, 8D, and regulatory investigations require direct traceability from customer case to the spe

Full narrative description of the customer issue, request, or complaint as reported by the customer. For quality complaints, includes the customer's account of the product defect, observed symptoms, and business impact.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Description; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2

description: Externally visible, human-readable case reference number used in all customer communications and cross-system references. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Case Number field.

Current workflow state of the case lifecycle. Open: newly created, awaiting assignment. In Progress: actively being worked. Pending Customer: awaiting customer response or information. Resolved: solution provided, awaiting confirmation. Closed: fully completed and confirmed.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Status; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2

description: Classification of the customer case by business function. Technical Support covers formulation and application assistance; Order Issue covers shipment discrepancies and billing disputes; SDS (Safety Data Sheet) Request and COA (Certificate of Analysis) Request cover documentation fulfillment; Regulatory Inquiry covers REACH, TSCA, GHS compliance questions; Quality Complaint covers OOS (Out of Specification) product issues.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Type; GHS; REACH; TSCA; ISO 9001

description: The communication channel through which the customer submitted or initiated the case. Supports channel analytics and resource allocation for customer service operations.
The date on which the case was fully closed after customer confirmation of resolution. Distinct from resolved_date; a case may be resolved but remain open pending customer acknowledgment.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Closed Date; ISO 9001

description: Reference number of the Certificate of Analysis (COA) document associated with the product lot involved in the case. Used for quality complaints to cross-reference the analytical results at time of shipment against the customer's reported issue.
reference: LabWare LIMS COA; ISO 9001 Clause 8.6; GMP

description: The date and time when the case record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit trail creation marker and is used for SLA calculation baseline.

Indicates whether a financial credit note has been issued to the customer as part of the resolution of a quality complaint or order discrepancy case. When True, a corresponding credit memo exists in SAP FI/CO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Credit Memo; SAP SD; SOX

description: The SAP document number of the credit note issued to the customer for a quality complaint or order discrepancy resolution. Populated only when credit_note_issued is True. Enables financial reconciliation between the case and the accounting entry.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; SAP SD Credit Memo; SOX; FASB

description: The native case identifier from Salesforce CRM, the system of record for customer cases. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational CRM system.

Categorical outcome of the customer satisfaction assessment following case closure. Derived from the customer's qualitative feedback or survey response. Complements the numeric customer_satisfaction_rating for segmentation and reporting.
reference: Salesforce CRM; ISO 9001 Clause 9.1.2; ACC Responsible Care

description: Numeric customer satisfaction score collected after case resolution, typically on a 1-5 scale (1=Very Dissatisfied, 5=Very Satisfied). Sourced from post-resolution customer survey. Used as a KPI (Key Performance Indicator) for customer service quality.

Reference number of the 8D (Eight Disciplines) problem-solving report generated for a quality complaint case. The 8D methodology is the standard structured problem-solving workflow for quality complaints in chemical manufacturing. Populated when an 8D report has been formally initiated.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 10.2; SAP QM; AIAG 8D Standard; ACC Responsible Care

description: Indicates whether the case has been formally escalated beyond the initially assigned service representative, either due to SLA breach, severity, or customer request. Escalated cases trigger management notification workflows.
The date on which the case was formally escalated. Populated only when escalated is True. Used to measure escalation response time and identify systemic SLA failure patterns.

The reason the case was escalated. SLA Breach: resolution deadline exceeded. Customer Request: customer demanded escalation. Safety Concern: potential EHS (Environment Health and Safety) implication. Regulatory Risk: potential regulatory non-compliance. Management Directive: escalated by management decision.

business_glossary_term: Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Investigation ID
description: The investigation record identifier in LabWare LIMS linked to this case for OOS (Out of Specification) product investigations. Populated when an OOS investigation has been initiated in the LIMS system in response to a quality complaint.
reference: LabWare LIMS OOS Investigation; ISO 9001 Clause 8.7; GMP

description: The date and time when the case record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks lakehouse.

Indicates whether the quality complaint involves a product that has been confirmed as Out of Specification (OOS) against the product's Certificate of Analysis (COA) or specification sheet. When True, triggers mandatory OOS investigation workflow in LabWare LIMS.
reference: LabWare LIMS OOS Investigation; ISO 9001 Clause 8.7; GMP; FDA

description: The calendar date on which the customer case was officially opened and entered into the system. Used for SLA (Service Level Agreement) calculation and aging analysis.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Created Date; ISO 9001

description: Business priority level assigned to the case, driving SLA (Service Level Agreement) response and resolution targets. Critical priority is reserved for safety-related quality complaints or regulatory non-compliance issues.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Priority; OSHA PSM; ISO 9001

description: Current status of the Root Cause Investigation (RCI) triggered by a quality complaint. Tracks whether the investigation into the product defect or OOS (Out of Specification) condition has been initiated, is ongoing, or has been completed. Applicable only to quality_complaint case type.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM; LabWare LIMS; ISO 9001 Clause 10.2; CAPA

description: The specific regulatory framework or standard that is the subject of a regulatory inquiry case. Identifies whether the customer's inquiry relates to REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals), TSCA (Toxic Substances Control Act), GHS (Globally Harmonized System), or other applicable regulation. Populated for regulatory_inquiry case type.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; TSCA; GHS; ECHA; EPA

description: Detailed narrative of the actions taken and solution provided to resolve the customer case. For quality complaints, includes findings from the RCI (Root Cause Investigation) and corrective actions applied.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Resolution; ISO 9001 Clause 8.7; CAPA

description: The date on which the case was marked as resolved, meaning the solution or response was provided to the customer. Used to calculate actual resolution time against SLA targets.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Closed Date; ISO 9001

description: The document control number of the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) provided or referenced in response to an SDS request case. Enables traceability of which SDS version was fulfilled. Applicable to sds_request case type.
reference: GHS (Globally Harmonized System) SDS Requirements; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200; REACH Article 31; ECHA

description: Severity classification of the issue impact on the customer's operations or safety. Distinct from priority (which drives internal response urgency), severity reflects the actual business or safety impact of the reported issue. Critical severity triggers mandatory CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) and RCI (Root Cause Investigation) workflows.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 8.7; SAP QM Defect Classification; OSHA

description: Indicates whether the case resolution exceeded the SLA due date, constituting an SLA breach. Used for service quality reporting, customer penalty tracking, and continuous improvement initiatives.
The contractual or policy-defined deadline by which the case must be resolved, based on case type, priority, and applicable customer SLA agreement. Drives escalation workflows when breached.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case SLA; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this case record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage; SAP S/4HANA; Salesforce CRM; LabWare LIMS

description: Brief, descriptive title summarizing the customer issue or request. Used as the primary display label in case management queues and customer communications.
Unique surrogate identifier for each customer interaction record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the interaction data product.
Reference to the customer (account) involved in this interaction. Links to the customer master data product.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Object; SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master

description: Reference to the customer service case or complaint record linked to this interaction, if applicable. Supports complaint resolution tracking and Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) workflows.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Object; Intelex EHS CAPA Module

description: Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Supports support interaction logs tying each customer interaction to the specific chemical product discussed, required for service quality metrics.
Reference to the specific customer contact person who participated in this interaction. Links to the customer contact data product.
Reference to the specific customer site or plant location where the interaction occurred or is relevant to. Supports site-level account management and technical service tracking.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Site; SAP S/4HANA SD Ship-To Party

description: Reference to the sales opportunity linked to this interaction, if applicable. Enables tracking of interactions that advance a specific sales pursuit.

Foreign key linking to order.order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Interaction Log per Order ties customer/technical interactions to a specific sales order, enabling SLA measurement and order‑level support metrics.

Foreign key linking to sales.quote. Business justification: Customer interactions in chemical sales frequently involve quote presentations, price negotiations, and quote follow-ups. Linking interaction to quote enables CRM tracking of which quotes were discuss

Foreign key linking to ehs.safety_data_sheet. Business justification: SDS request fulfillment process: customer interactions flagged sds_request_flag=true represent formal SDS distribution events (OSHA HazCom 2012, REACH). Linking the interaction to the specific SDS doc
The communication channel through which the interaction was conducted. Supports omnichannel analytics and customer engagement reporting.
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity Channel; ISO 9001 Customer Communication

description: Indicates whether the customer requested a Certificate of Analysis (COA) during this interaction. Supports quality document fulfillment tracking and customer quality management.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management; LabWare LIMS COA Module

description: Indicates whether this interaction involves or originated from a customer complaint. Triggers linkage to the complaint/case management workflow and Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) process.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1.2 Customer Satisfaction; Salesforce CRM Case

description: The date and time when this interaction record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: Salesforce CRM CreatedDate; SOX Section 404 Audit Trail

description: External identifier from Salesforce CRM activity record. Used to trace the interaction back to the system of record for reconciliation and lineage.
Number of customer-side contacts who participated in the interaction. Indicates the breadth of customer engagement and stakeholder involvement.
Comma-separated list of customer contact names or identifiers who participated in the interaction. Confidential business relationship data.
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity Invitees; GDPR Article 4

description: Numeric satisfaction rating provided by the customer or assessed by the account manager following the interaction, typically on a 1-5 or 1-10 scale. Supports Key Performance Indicator (KPI) tracking for customer relationship health.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1.2 Customer Satisfaction; ACC Responsible Care

description: The total elapsed time of the interaction in minutes. Used to assess engagement depth, resource utilization, and account planning efficiency.
The target date by which the required follow-up action must be completed. Used for task management and account manager accountability tracking.
Free-text description of the specific follow-up actions committed to during the interaction. Captures commitments made to the customer for accountability and continuity.
Indicates whether a follow-up action or subsequent interaction is required as a result of this interaction. Drives task creation and account planning workflows.
The calendar date on which the customer interaction occurred. Used for day-level scheduling, account planning, and reporting.
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity Date; ISO 9001 Customer Communication Records

description: Detailed narrative of the interaction content, discussion points, and context. Captures technical topics discussed, products referenced, and any regulatory or compliance matters raised.
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity Description; ISO 9001 Customer Communication Records

description: Current lifecycle status of the interaction record. Indicates whether the interaction was completed as planned, cancelled, or requires rescheduling.
The precise date and time at which the customer interaction commenced. Supports time-zone-aware analytics and SLA measurement.

Classification of the nature or purpose of the customer interaction. Distinguishes between sales-driven, technical service, complaint handling, and digital engagement touchpoints. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sales_call|technical_visit|complaint_call|email_exchange|webinar|trade_show|portal_login|video_conference — promote to reference product]
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity Type; ACC Responsible Care Customer Engagement Guidelines

description: Number of internal Chemical Mfg employees who participated in the interaction. Used for resource utilization and engagement intensity analysis.

Comma-separated list of internal employee names or identifiers who participated in the interaction (e.g., account manager, technical service representative, application chemist). Supports multi-participant interaction tracking.

Physical or virtual location where the interaction took place (e.g., customer plant site, trade show venue, video conference URL, Chemical Mfg office). Relevant for in-person and on-site technical visits.
The date and time when this interaction record was last updated in the system. Supports change tracking, data quality monitoring, and audit compliance.
reference: Salesforce CRM LastModifiedDate; SOX Section 404 Audit Trail

description: The scheduled date for the next planned customer interaction as agreed during this touchpoint. Supports account planning cadence and proactive customer engagement management.
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity; ACC Responsible Care Account Planning

description: The result or disposition of the interaction as assessed by the internal participant. Supports win/loss analysis, customer satisfaction tracking, and account health scoring.
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity Result; ACC Responsible Care Customer Satisfaction

description: Business priority level assigned to the interaction, reflecting urgency of follow-up or strategic importance of the customer engagement.

The Chemical Mfg product line or portfolio segment discussed during the interaction (e.g., performance materials, agricultural solutions, specialty chemicals). Supports product-level engagement analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Product Hierarchy; Salesforce CRM Product

description: Indicates whether the interaction involved discussion of regulatory or compliance topics such as REACH, TSCA, GHS, SDS requirements, or EPA/OSHA obligations. Supports regulatory engagement tracking and compliance reporting.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; TSCA; EPA; OSHA; GHS

description: The sales territory code associated with this interaction, derived from the account owner's territory assignment. Supports territory-level performance reporting and account planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Sales Territory; Salesforce CRM Territory Management

description: Indicates whether the customer requested a product sample during this interaction. Triggers linkage to the sample request fulfillment process in the sales domain.
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity; SAP S/4HANA SD Sample Management

description: Indicates whether the customer requested a Safety Data Sheet (SDS) or Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) during this interaction. Supports regulatory document fulfillment tracking per GHS and OSHA HazCom requirements.
reference: OSHA HazCom Standard 29 CFR 1910.1200; GHS; REACH Article 31

description: The originating operational system from which this interaction record was sourced. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and multi-system integration governance.
reference: Salesforce CRM; SAP S/4HANA SD; Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage

description: Brief title or subject line summarizing the topic of the interaction. Mirrors the Salesforce CRM activity subject field and supports keyword search and categorization.

The primary technical subject matter discussed during the interaction (e.g., formulation support, application development, regulatory compliance, Safety Data Sheet (SDS) review, Certificate of Analysis (COA) query). Supports technical service analytics.
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity; ACC Responsible Care Technical Service

description: Unique identifier for the chemical product. Primary key for the chemical product master record. This is the single source of truth for product identity across all enterprise systems.

Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.cas_registry. Business justification: Links product to authoritative CAS registry for hazard classification, REACH/TSCA reporting, and consistent chemical identification.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for product cost allocation and profitability reporting; finance tracks manufacturing costs per cost center.

Foreign key linking to ehs.facility. Business justification: Chemical products are manufactured at specific facilities. Facility-level TRI, RMP, and EPCRA regulatory reporting requires knowing which facility produces each chemical product. A chemical manufactur
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Links product to default GL revenue account for journal entries; required for accurate posting of sales transactions.

Foreign key linking to inventory.hazmat_storage_rule. Business justification: Each chemical product's GHS hazard class and packing group maps to a specific hazmat storage rule governing segregation, temperature limits, and ventilation requirements. This link enables automated s

Foreign key linking to product.hierarchy. Business justification: chemical_product has denormalized `product_line` and `business_unit` STRING fields. The hierarchy table is the authoritative multi-level product classification for commercial reporting, portfolio mana
Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Tracks R&D or capital project costs per product; internal orders are used to capture development expenditures.

Foreign key linking to maintenance.plant. Business justification: Chemical products are manufactured/stored at specific plants. Plant assignment drives site-specific regulatory compliance (EPA/OSHA permits), emergency response planning, and production capacity repor

Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Procurement Planning uses a designated primary vendor per chemical product to generate purchase orders, cost analysis, and supplier performance reporting.
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Enables revenue attribution and margin analysis per profit center, essential for product-level financial statements.
Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Required for Regional Pricing and Regulatory Reporting linking product to its default sales territory.
Recommended use cases and application instructions for the product. Describes target industries, processes, and best practices for product utilization.
Primary unit of measure for inventory, sales, and production transactions. KG: kilogram, L: liter, MT: metric ton, GAL: gallon, LB: pound, TON: short ton.
IUPAC systematic chemical name or common chemical name describing the molecular composition. Used for regulatory reporting and technical documentation.
Timestamp when the product master record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and new product tracking.
Product density in kilograms per liter at standard temperature and pressure. Used for volume-to-weight conversions in logistics and formulation calculations.
Date the product was or will be withdrawn from active sale. Null for active products. Used for end-of-life planning and inventory liquidation.

business_glossary_term: Food and Drug Administration (FDA) Approval Required
description: Indicates whether the product requires FDA approval for food-contact, pharmaceutical, or cosmetic applications. True if FDA oversight applies.
Lowest temperature at which vapors ignite when exposed to an ignition source. Critical for flammability classification, storage requirements, and fire safety planning.
GHS hazard classification codes (e.g., H226 Flammable liquid, H302 Harmful if swallowed). Used for SDS authoring, label generation, and workplace safety communication.
Comma-separated list of GHS pictogram codes required on product labels (e.g., GHS02 Flame, GHS07 Exclamation Mark). Communicates hazards visually per global standards.
Percentage of EPA-listed hazardous air pollutants by weight. Triggers additional reporting and control requirements under Clean Air Act regulations.
DOT hazard class for transportation (e.g., Class 3 Flammable Liquids, Class 8 Corrosives). Determines shipping requirements, packaging specifications, and carrier restrictions.

Current stage in the product lifecycle. Active: available for sale. Discontinued: no longer manufactured but may have inventory. Developmental: in R&D phase. Phased Out: transitioning to discontinuation. Pending Approval: awaiting regulatory or internal approval. Obsolete: fully retired.
Published list price per base unit in USD before discounts or negotiations. Starting point for customer pricing discussions. Confidential business data.
SAP Material Master number. The authoritative business identifier for the product in SAP S/4HANA MM module. Used across procurement, production, sales, and inventory transactions.
Standard packaging options available for the product (e.g., 55-gallon drum, 1000L IBC tote, bulk tanker, 25kg bag). Affects pricing, logistics, and customer ordering.
DOT packing group indicating degree of danger. I: high danger, II: medium danger, III: low danger. Determines packaging performance standards for hazmat transport.
pH measurement indicating acidity or alkalinity on a 0-14 scale. Critical for corrosivity assessment, handling requirements, and application compatibility.

Official commercial product name as marketed to customers. Primary human-readable identifier used in sales literature, technical data sheets, and customer communications.

Classification of product by commercial offering model. Bulk: high-volume commodity. Packaged: pre-packaged finished goods. Specialty: high-value low-volume. Custom Blend: customer-specific formulation. Intermediate: used in further manufacturing.

Standard manufacturing cost per base unit in USD. Used for inventory valuation, margin analysis, and financial reporting. Confidential business data.

Date and time when this hierarchy node record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.

description: Business identifier code for the hierarchy node. Used in SAP S/4HANA product hierarchy configuration and external reporting systems. Alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the node within the hierarchy structure.

Reference to the previous specification version that this version replaces. Nullable for initial specifications. Supports version history tracking and regulatory audit trail for specification changes.

Upper acceptance limit for the quality parameter. Test results above this value indicate Out of Specification (OOS) condition. Nullable for parameters with only lower limits.

description: Additional comments, instructions, or context for this specification parameter. May include special handling instructions, historical context, or clarifications for laboratory technicians and Quality Control (QC) personnel.

Name of the quality parameter being specified. Examples include assay, pH (Potential of Hydrogen), viscosity, moisture content, color, density, particle size, heavy metals concentration, residual solvents, Volatile Organic Compound (VOC) content, and other chemical or physical properties.

True if regulatory-mandated, false if internal quality control only.

Classification of the specification based on its purpose in the quality control lifecycle. Release specifications define final product acceptance criteria; in-process specifications govern intermediate checkpoints; shelf-life and stability specifications define storage and aging limits.

description: Ideal or nominal value for the quality parameter. Used for Statistical Process Control (SPC) and process optimization. Nullable for parameters defined only by acceptance ranges.

Unit in which the parameter value is expressed. Examples include Parts Per Million (PPM), percent, pH units, centipoise (cP) for viscosity, grams per cubic centimeter (g/cm³) for density, microns for particle size. Must align with test method reference.

description: Classification of the rebate mechanism applicable to this distributor agreement: volume_tiered (rebate percentage increases with purchase volume tiers), flat_rate (fixed percentage on all purchases), growth_incentive (rebate tied to year-over-year growth targets), or none (no rebate applicable).

description: Indicates whether the distributor is contractually required to adhere to the ACC (American Chemistry Council) Responsible Care program standards as a condition of this distribution agreement.

description: SAP S/4HANA SD contract document number (transaction VA41/VA42) corresponding to this distributor agreement. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the CRM pipeline and ERP order management.

Indicates whether the distributor is contractually obligated to distribute Safety Data Sheets (SDS) to downstream customers for all chemical products sold under this agreement. Mandatory under GHS, REACH, and OSHA HazCom 2012 (29 CFR 1910.1200).

description: Date on which both parties executed (signed) the distributor agreement. May differ from effective_date when agreements are backdated or post-dated.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Contract Management; SOX Section 302

description: Actual date on which the agreement was terminated early (before natural expiry). Null if the agreement has not been terminated. Distinct from expiry_date which represents the scheduled end date.

description: Externally-known business identifier for the pricing agreement, typically matching the SAP SD condition contract number or Salesforce CRM contract number. Used in customer-facing communications and purchase orders.

description: Current lifecycle state of the pricing agreement. Draft = under preparation; Pending Approval = submitted for internal authorization; Active = in force and applicable to orders; Suspended = temporarily halted; Expired = past effective end date; Terminated = ended before expiry by mutual or unilateral action; Cancelled = voided before activation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|suspended|expired|terminated|cancelled — promote to reference product]

description: Classification of the pricing agreement by commercial structure. Contract Price = fixed negotiated unit price; Volume Rebate = retrospective rebate based on volume thresholds; Long-Term Supply = multi-year supply commitment with pricing; Spot Price = short-term market-based pricing; Distributor Price = channel partner pricing. description: Name or employee ID of the internal approver (e.g., Sales Director, Commercial Manager) who authorized this pricing agreement. Required for SOX audit trail and pricing governance.

description: SAP SD distribution channel through which the product is sold under this agreement. Direct = sold directly to end customer; Distributor = sold through channel partner; Toll Manufacturing = customer-owned feedstock processing; Export = cross-border sales.

description: Minimum order quantity (MOQ) the customer must place per order to qualify for the contracted price. Expressed in the price UOM. Below this threshold, standard list pricing may apply.

description: Indicates whether the agreement contains a price escalation clause allowing periodic price adjustments based on raw material indices, energy costs, or inflation benchmarks (e.g., CPI, feedstock index). True = escalation clause present.

description: The quantity denominator for the unit price (e.g., price per 100 KG, price per 1000 LB). Corresponds to SAP SD condition pricing quantity (KONP.KPEIN). Enables accurate per-unit price calculation when price is expressed per batch or bulk quantity.

Unit of measure to which the base unit price applies (e.g., KG = kilogram, MT = metric ton, LB = pound, L = liter, GAL = gallon, EA = each, BAG = bag, DR = drum, IBC = intermediate bulk container). description: Indicates whether the products covered by this pricing agreement are compliant with EU REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals) regulation. description: Defines how the pricing agreement is renewed at expiry. Auto Renew = automatically extends for the same term unless cancelled; Manual Renew = requires explicit renegotiation; No Renewal = single-term agreement with no renewal provision.

description: The SAP SD condition contract number (KONA.KNUMA_AG) that is the system-of-record identifier for this pricing agreement in SAP S/4HANA. Enables traceability back to the operational source system.

description: Type of surcharge applicable to this agreement in addition to the base unit price. The fiscal quarter to which this target applies (Q1–Q4). Populated only when period_granularity is 'quarterly'. Null for annual or monthly targets. Supports quarterly sales performance reviews and incentive compensation calculations.

The fiscal year to which this sales target applies, expressed as a four-digit integer (e.g., 2025). Aligns with Chemical Mfg's SAP FI/CO fiscal year calendar for financial reporting and EBITDA planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; FASB ASC 280; SOX

description: The minimum revenue threshold (floor) below which no incentive compensation is earned. Typically 70–80% of the base target. Used in incentive compensation calculations to define the minimum attainment level required for commission eligibility.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; Salesforce CRM; SOX

description: The geographic sales region to which this target applies (e.g., North America, EMEA, APAC, LATAM). Aligns with SAP SD sales organization regional hierarchy and Salesforce CRM territory management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: north_america|emea|apac|latam|mea|greater_china — promote to reference product]

The planned year-over-year or period-over-period growth rate embedded in this target, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0800 = 8.00% growth). Derived from strategic planning assumptions and used to validate target reasonableness in management reviews.

The date on which this target was most recently revised or adjusted. Mid-year target revisions may occur due to market conditions, M&A activity, or force majeure events. Supports audit trail for target change management.
reference: SOX; Salesforce CRM; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: The planned gross margin percentage target for the period, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.3250 = 32.50%). Used for profitability management and incentive compensation plans that include margin-based components. Populated when target_type is 'margin'.

Free-text field for additional context, assumptions, or qualifications associated with this sales target. May include notes on market conditions, excluded accounts, special pricing agreements, or target-setting assumptions documented by the sales manager.
The calendar date on which the target measurement period ends. For annual targets this is typically December 31 or the fiscal year end date. Aligns with SAP FI/CO posting period close dates.

The time granularity at which this target is set and tracked. Annual targets cover a full fiscal year; quarterly targets align to SAP posting periods Q1–Q4; monthly targets align to individual SAP posting periods for detailed attainment tracking.
The calendar date on which the target measurement period begins. For annual targets this is typically January 1 or the fiscal year start date. Aligns with SAP FI/CO posting period open dates.

The chemical product category or market segment covered by this target (e.g., Specialty Chemicals, Agrochemicals, Performance Polymers, Industrial Chemicals, Coatings & Adhesives). Sourced from SAP MM material group classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: specialty_chemicals|agrochemicals|performance_polymers|industrial_chemicals|coatings_adhesives|plastics|solvents|catalysts — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; ACC Responsible Care; TSCA

description: The ratio of actual revenue to target revenue for the period, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9250 = 92.50% attainment). Calculated as actual_revenue_usd / target_revenue_usd. Used for sales performance management, KPI dashboards, and incentive compensation calculations. NOTE: This is a stored attainment snapshot, not a real-time computed metric, to support point-in-time reporting and audit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; Salesforce CRM; SOX

description: The planned revenue target value denominated in US Dollars (USD) for the defined period. Represents the gross revenue goal before deductions. Used for EBITDA planning, COGS analysis, and sales performance management. Populated when target_type is 'revenue' or 'margin'.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; FASB ASC 606; SOX

description: The business reason for the most recent revision to this target. Market_conditions covers demand shifts; portfolio_change covers product additions/removals; territory_realignment covers sales org restructuring; acquisition covers M&A impacts; force_majeure covers unforeseeable events; correction covers data entry errors.

The commercial channel through which sales against this target are expected to be realized. Direct indicates sales to end-use customers; distributor covers third-party distribution partners; OEM covers original equipment manufacturer accounts; ecommerce covers digital order channels; agent covers commissioned sales agents.

The methodology used to establish this sales target. Top_down targets are allocated from corporate/BU level; bottom_up targets are built from rep-level forecasts; negotiated targets result from manager-rep agreement; statistical targets are derived from historical trend analysis and SPC methods.

The aspirational upper-bound revenue target (stretch goal) in USD, set above the base target to incentivize overperformance. Typically 110–120% of the base target. Used in incentive compensation plans to calculate accelerated commission rates for above-target performance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; Salesforce CRM; SOX

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the sales target record, used in management reporting, incentive compensation plans, and cross-system references between SAP SD and Salesforce CRM.

Human-readable descriptive name for the sales target, such as 'FY2025 Q1 Specialty Chemicals - North America' or 'Annual Volume Target - Agrochemicals - Rep Smith'. Used in dashboards and management reports.

Current lifecycle state of the sales target record. Draft targets are under review; active targets are in-period and being tracked; approved targets have passed management sign-off; closed targets have reached period end; cancelled targets were withdrawn before period start.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; Salesforce CRM; SOX

description: Classification of the sales target by the primary metric being tracked. Revenue targets are denominated in USD; volume targets in MT or kg; margin targets as gross margin percentage; new_business for new customer acquisition; account_growth for existing account expansion.

description: The timestamp when this sales target record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Updated on any change to target values, status, or attainment data. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse pipeline.

The ratio of actual volume to target volume for the period, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 1.0500 = 105.00% attainment). Calculated as actual_volume_mt / target_volume_mt. Used for supply chain planning alignment and sales volume KPI reporting.

The planned sales volume target expressed in metric tons (MT). Used for chemical manufacturing capacity planning, raw material procurement forecasting, and supply chain management. Populated when target_type is 'volume'. Aligns with SAP SD delivery quantity planning.

description: The unit of measure for the volume target. MT (metric tons) is standard for bulk chemicals; KG for specialty/fine chemicals; L (liters) for liquid products; GAL (gallons) for US market; LB (pounds) for certain North American contracts. Aligns with SAP MM base unit of measure.

Foreign key linking to maintenance.plant. Business justification: Sales territories in chemical manufacturing are served by specific manufacturing plants, driving delivery lead time commitments, freight cost estimates, and regional supply planning. Territory-to-plan
